Shadow Covering - 3x3 square, sun straight down

View Code (12271 chars)
use std::io::{self, BufRead};
use nalgebra::{Point2, Point3, Vector2, Vector3, UnitQuaternion, Rotation2};

struct TetrahedronTransform {
    position: Point3<f64>,
    rotation: UnitQuaternion<f64>,
    scale: f64,
}

fn main() {
    let (polygon_vertices, sun_direction) = parse_input();

    if polygon_vertices.len() < 3 {
        println!("0");
        return;
    }

    let triangles = triangulate_polygon(&polygon_vertices);

    let tetrahedrons = find_covering_tetrahedrons(&triangles, &sun_direction, polygon_vertices.len());

    print_output(&tetrahedrons);
}

fn parse_input() -> (Vec<Point2<f64>>, Vector3<f64>) {
    let stdin = io::stdin();
    let mut lines = stdin.lock().lines();

    let nv: usize = lines.next().and_then(|l| l.ok()?.parse().ok()).unwrap_or(0);

    let mut vertices = Vec::with_capacity(nv);
    for _ in 0..nv {
        if let Some(Ok(line)) = lines.next() {
            let mut coords = line.split_whitespace();
            if let (Some(x_str), Some(y_str)) = (coords.next(), coords.next()) {
                if let (Ok(x), Ok(y)) = (x_str.parse(), y_str.parse()) {
                    vertices.push(Point2::new(x, y));
                }
            }
        }
    }

    let sun_direction = if let Some(Ok(line)) = lines.next() {
        let mut coords = line.split_whitespace();
        if let (Some(sx_str), Some(sy_str), Some(sz_str)) = (coords.next(), coords.next(), coords.next()) {
            if let (Ok(sx), Ok(sy), Ok(sz)) = (sx_str.parse(), sy_str.parse(), sz_str.parse()) {
                Vector3::new(sx, sy, sz)
            } else {
                Vector3::new(0.0, 0.0, -1.0)
            }
        } else {
            Vector3::new(0.0, 0.0, -1.0)
        }
    } else {
        Vector3::new(0.0, 0.0, -1.0)
    };
    
    (vertices, sun_direction)
}

fn triangulate_polygon(polygon_vertices: &Vec<Point2<f64>>) -> Vec<[Point2<f64>; 3]> {
    let vertices_flat: Vec<f64> = polygon_vertices.iter().flat_map(|p| vec![p.x, p.y]).collect();
    let Ok(indices) = earcutr::earcut(&vertices_flat, &[], 2) else {
        return Vec::new();
    };
    
    indices.chunks_exact(3).map(|chunk| {
        [
            polygon_vertices[chunk[0]],
            polygon_vertices[chunk[1]],
            polygon_vertices[chunk[2]],
        ]
    }).collect()
}

fn find_covering_tetrahedrons(
    triangles: &Vec<[Point2<f64>; 3]>,
    sun_direction: &Vector3<f64>,
    nv: usize,
) -> Vec<TetrahedronTransform> {
    if triangles.is_empty() {
        return Vec::new();
    }
    
    let std_tetra_verts = [
        Point3::new(1.0, 0.0, -0.707), Point3::new(-1.0, 0.0, -0.707),
        Point3::new(0.0, 1.0, 0.707), Point3::new(0.0, -1.0, 0.707),
    ];

    let shadow_points: Vec<Point2<f64>> = std_tetra_verts
        .iter()
        .map(|v| project_point(*v, sun_direction))
        .collect();

    let shadow_hull = convex_hull(&shadow_points);
    let shadow_centroid = calculate_centroid(&shadow_hull);

    if nv < 20 {
        cover_triangles_individually(triangles, &shadow_hull, shadow_centroid, sun_direction)
    } else {
        cover_points_greedily(triangles, &shadow_hull, shadow_centroid, sun_direction)
    }
}

fn cover_triangles_individually(
    triangles: &Vec<[Point2<f64>; 3]>,
    shadow_hull: &[Point2<f64>],
    shadow_centroid: Point2<f64>,
    sun_direction: &Vector3<f64>
) -> Vec<TetrahedronTransform> {
    let mut tetrahedrons = Vec::new();

    for (i, triangle) in triangles.iter().enumerate() {
        let triangle_centroid = (triangle[0].coords + triangle[1].coords + triangle[2].coords) / 3.0;
        
        let mut best_rotation_angle = 0.0;
        let mut min_scale = f64::MAX;

        for angle_step in 0..12 {
            let angle = (angle_step as f64) * std::f64::consts::PI / 6.0;
            let rotation = Rotation2::new(angle);
            
            let rotated_shadow_hull: Vec<Point2<f64>> = shadow_hull.iter().map(|p| rotation * (*p - shadow_centroid) + shadow_centroid.coords).collect();
            
            let mut required_scale = 0.0;
            for &vertex in triangle.iter() {
                required_scale = required_scale.max(find_scale_to_cover_point(vertex, &rotated_shadow_hull, triangle_centroid.into()));
            }

            if required_scale < min_scale {
                min_scale = required_scale;
                best_rotation_angle = angle;
            }
        }

        let scale = min_scale.min(10.0).max(0.1);
        let rotation_quat = UnitQuaternion::from_axis_angle(&Vector3::z_axis(), best_rotation_angle);
        let displacement = (i as f64) * 20.0 * sun_direction.normalize();
        
        let offset = Rotation2::new(best_rotation_angle) * (shadow_centroid.coords * scale);
        let position = Point3::new(triangle_centroid.x - offset.x, triangle_centroid.y - offset.y, 0.0) + displacement;

        tetrahedrons.push(TetrahedronTransform { position, rotation: rotation_quat, scale });
    }

    tetrahedrons
}

fn find_scale_to_cover_point(point: Point2<f64>, shadow_hull: &[Point2<f64>], shadow_center: Point2<f64>) -> f64 {
    let mut max_scale = 0.0;
    let point_vec = point - shadow_center;

    for i in 0..shadow_hull.len() {
        let p1 = shadow_hull[i] - shadow_center;
        let p2 = shadow_hull[(i + 1) % shadow_hull.len()] - shadow_center;

        let edge_vec = p2 - p1;
        let cross = edge_vec.perp(&point_vec);

        if cross.abs() > 1e-9 {
            let t = (p1).perp(&edge_vec) / cross;
            let u = (p1).perp(&point_vec) / cross;

            if t >= 0.0 && u >= 0.0 && u <= 1.0 {
                if t > 1e-9 {
                    let scale = 1.0/t;
                    if scale > max_scale {
                        max_scale = scale;
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }
    max_scale
}

fn cover_points_greedily(
    triangles: &Vec<[Point2<f64>; 3]>,
    shadow_hull: &[Point2<f64>],
    shadow_centroid: Point2<f64>,
    sun_direction: &Vector3<f64>
) -> Vec<TetrahedronTransform> {
    let mut tetrahedrons = Vec::new();
    let mut uncovered_points: Vec<Point2<f64>> = triangles.iter().flat_map(|t| t.to_vec()).collect();
    uncovered_points.sort_by(|a, b| a.x.partial_cmp(&b.x).unwrap().then(a.y.partial_cmp(&b.y).unwrap()));
    uncovered_points.dedup();

    let mut tetra_idx = 0;
    while !uncovered_points.is_empty() {
        let point_to_cover = uncovered_points[0];
        
        let mut best_transform = None;
        let mut max_covered_points = 0;

        for angle_step in 0..12 {
            let angle = (angle_step as f64) * std::f64::consts::PI / 6.0;
            for scale_step in 1..=10 {
                let scale = scale_step as f64;
                
                let rotation = Rotation2::new(angle);
                
                let transformed_shadow: Vec<Point2<f64>> = shadow_hull.iter().map(|p| {
                    point_to_cover + rotation * ((*p - shadow_centroid) * scale)
                }).collect();
                
                let mut covered_count = 0;
                for p in &uncovered_points {
                    if is_point_in_convex_polygon(*p, &transformed_shadow) {
                        covered_count += 1;
                    }
                }

                if covered_count > max_covered_points {
                    max_covered_points = covered_count;
                    let rotation_quat = UnitQuaternion::from_axis_angle(&Vector3::z_axis(), angle);
                    let displacement = (tetra_idx as f64) * 20.0 * sun_direction.normalize();

                    let offset = rotation * (shadow_centroid.coords * scale);
                    let position_3d = Point3::new(point_to_cover.x - offset.x, point_to_cover.y - offset.y, 0.0) + displacement;
                    
                    best_transform = Some(TetrahedronTransform { position: position_3d, rotation: rotation_quat, scale});
                }
            }
        }
        
        if let Some(transform) = best_transform {
            let rotation = Rotation2::new(transform.rotation.angle());
            let center_2d = project_point(transform.position, sun_direction) + (rotation * (shadow_centroid.coords * transform.scale));

            let transformed_shadow: Vec<Point2<f64>> = shadow_hull.iter().map(|p| {
                center_2d + rotation * ((*p - shadow_centroid) * transform.scale)
            }).collect();

            uncovered_points.retain(|p| !is_point_in_convex_polygon(*p, &transformed_shadow));
            tetrahedrons.push(transform);
            tetra_idx += 1;
        } else {
            uncovered_points.remove(0);
        }
    }

    tetrahedrons
}

fn print_output(tetrahedrons: &Vec<TetrahedronTransform>) {
    println!("{}", tetrahedrons.len());
    for t in tetrahedrons {
        println!(
            "{} {} {} {} {} {} {} {}",
            t.position.x, t.position.y, t.position.z,
            t.rotation.w, t.rotation.i, t.rotation.j, t.rotation.k,
            t.scale
        );
    }
}

fn project_point(point: Point3<f64>, sun_direction: &Vector3<f64>) -> Point2<f64> {
    if sun_direction.z.abs() < 1e-9 {
        return Point2::new(point.x, point.y);
    }
    let t = -point.z / sun_direction.z;
    Point2::new(point.x + t * sun_direction.x, point.y + t * sun_direction.y)
}

fn convex_hull(points: &[Point2<f64>]) -> Vec<Point2<f64>> {
    if points.len() <= 3 {
        let mut unique_points = points.to_vec();
        unique_points.sort_by(|a, b| a.x.partial_cmp(&b.x).unwrap().then(a.y.partial_cmp(&b.y).unwrap()));
        unique_points.dedup();
        return unique_points;
    }

    let mut points = points.to_vec();
    let mut start_idx = 0;
    for i in 1..points.len() {
        if points[i].y < points[start_idx].y || (points[i].y == points[start_idx].y && points[i].x < points[start_idx].x) {
            start_idx = i;
        }
    }
    points.swap(0, start_idx);
    let start_point = points[0];

    points[1..].sort_unstable_by(|a, b| {
        cross_product_orientation(start_point, *a, *b)
            .map(|ordering| ordering.reverse())
            .unwrap_or_else(|| {
                let d1 = (a.x - start_point.x).powi(2) + (a.y - start_point.y).powi(2);
                let d2 = (b.x - start_point.x).powi(2) + (b.y - start_point.y).powi(2);
                d1.partial_cmp(&d2).unwrap()
            })
    });

    let mut hull = vec![points[0]];
    for &point in &points[1..] {
        while hull.len() >= 2 && cross_product_orientation(hull[hull.len()-2], hull[hull.len()-1], point) != Some(std::cmp::Ordering::Less) {
            hull.pop();
        }
        hull.push(point);
    }
    hull
}

fn cross_product_orientation(a: Point2<f64>, b: Point2<f64>, c: Point2<f64>) -> Option<std::cmp::Ordering> {
    let val = (b.x - a.x) * (c.y - a.y) - (b.y - a.y) * (c.x - a.x);
    val.partial_cmp(&0.0)
}

fn calculate_centroid(polygon: &[Point2<f64>]) -> Point2<f64> {
    if polygon.is_empty() { return Point2::new(0.0, 0.0); }
    let mut centroid = Vector2::new(0.0, 0.0);
    let mut signed_area = 0.0;
    
    for i in 0..polygon.len() {
        let p0 = polygon[i];
        let p1 = polygon[(i + 1) % polygon.len()];
        let cross = p0.x * p1.y - p1.x * p0.y;
        signed_area += cross;
        centroid.x += (p0.x + p1.x) * cross;
        centroid.y += (p0.y + p1.y) * cross;
    }

    if signed_area.abs() < 1e-9 {
        let mut sum = Vector2::new(0.0, 0.0);
        for p in polygon { sum += p.coords; }
        return if polygon.is_empty() { Point2::new(0.0,0.0) } else { (sum / polygon.len() as f64).into() };
    }
    
    signed_area *= 0.5;
    centroid /= (6.0 * signed_area);
    centroid.into()
}

fn is_point_in_convex_polygon(point: Point2<f64>, polygon: &[Point2<f64>]) -> bool {
    if polygon.len() < 3 { return false; }
    let mut first_sign = None;
    for i in 0..polygon.len() {
        let p1 = polygon[i];
        let p2 = polygon[(i + 1) % polygon.len()];
        let orient = cross_product_orientation(p1, p2, point);
        if orient == Some(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal) { return true; }

        if first_sign.is_none() {
            first_sign = orient;
        } else if first_sign != orient {
            return false;
        }
    }
    true
}

Test 16: Job-Shop Scheduling (C++) Score: 0.00 / 14
Typical Prompt:
You are solving a Job-Shop Scheduling problem in C++.

You must write a C++ solver that can handle ANY problem size from trivial to extremely large scale:
- **Trivial**: 2-3 jobs, 2-3 machines, 5-10 tasks total
- **Extreme**: 1000+ jobs, 50+ machines, 50k+ tasks total

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: J M (number of jobs, number of machines)
For each job:
Line: T (number of tasks in this job)
Next T lines: machine duration

**Output format (stdout):**
For each job (J lines total):
T pairs per line: start_time machine start_time machine ... (all tasks for that job)

**Example:**
Input:
2 2
2
0 3
1 2
2
1 2
0 4

Output:
0 0 3 1
0 1 5 0

(Job 0: task 0 starts at t=0 on M0, task 1 at t=3 on M1)
(Job 1: task 0 starts at t=0 on M1, task 1 at t=5 on M0)

**Key Constraints:**
1. Each task in a job must run on its specified machine
2. Tasks within a job must execute in order (task i before task i+1)
3. A machine can only run one task at a time
4. Tasks cannot be interrupted once started

**Environment:**

Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

Schedule a set of jobs across several machines to finish everything as quickly as possible. Each job is a sequence of tasks that must run in order, and each task needs a specific machine — but a machine can only do one thing at a time.

This classic factory-scheduling problem is NP-hard. Subpasses increase the number of jobs and machines. The baseline greedily starts whichever task can begin earliest.

Typical Prompt:
You are solving a 3D Bin Packing problem with axis-aligned boxes in C#.

Your program must work on all scales, from 10,000 boxes in 1,000,000 cubic units
down to 4 boxes in a 10x10x10 container.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: N W H D (number of boxes, container width, height, depth)
Next N lines: w h d (box dimensions)

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: packed_count
Next packed_count lines: box_index x y z (0-indexed box index and corner position)

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on container size and number of boxes
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 30 seconds even for very large containers
3. **Quality**: Maximize number of boxes packed while ensuring valid placements

**Constraints:**
- Boxes must be entirely within container bounds
- No two boxes may overlap
- Boxes are axis-aligned (no arbitrary rotation, but you may reorder w/h/d)
- Maximize number of boxes packed

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

Pack a collection of rectangular boxes into a shipping container, fitting as much total volume in as possible. Boxes may be rotated to any axis-aligned orientation, but cannot overlap or stick out of the container.

This 3D packing problem is NP-hard. Subpasses increase the number and variety of box sizes. The baseline uses a greedy first-fit approach.

3D AABB Packing - 4 boxes in 10x10x10

View Code (4248 chars)
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Linq;

public class BinPacking
{
    public struct Dimensions
    {
        public int W, H, D;
        public Dimensions(int w, int h, int d) { W = w; H = h; D = d; }
    }

    public struct Box
    {
        public int Id;
        public Dimensions Dim;
        public int Volume => Dim.W * Dim.H * Dim.D;
        public int MaxDim => Math.Max(Dim.W, Math.Max(Dim.H, Dim.D));

        public IEnumerable<Dimensions> GetRotations()
        {
            yield return new Dimensions(Dim.W, Dim.H, Dim.D);
            yield return new Dimensions(Dim.W, Dim.D, Dim.H);
            yield return new Dimensions(Dim.H, Dim.W, Dim.D);
            yield return new Dimensions(Dim.H, Dim.D, Dim.W);
            yield return new Dimensions(Dim.D, Dim.W, Dim.H);
            yield return new Dimensions(Dim.D, Dim.H, Dim.W);
        }
    }

    public struct PackedBox
    {
        public int Id;
        public int X, Y, Z;
        public Dimensions Dim;

        public PackedBox(int id, int x, int y, int z, Dimensions dim)
        {
            Id = id;
            X = x;
            Y = y;
            Z = z;
            Dim = dim;
        }
    }

    public static void Main(string[] args)
    {
        string[] firstLine = Console.ReadLine().Split(' ');
        int n = int.Parse(firstLine[0]);
        int containerW = int.Parse(firstLine[1]);
        int containerH = int.Parse(firstLine[2]);
        int containerD = int.Parse(firstLine[3]);

        var boxes = new List<Box>(n);
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
        {
            string[] boxLine = Console.ReadLine().Split(' ');
            boxes.Add(new Box
            {
                Id = i,
                Dim = new Dimensions(
                    int.Parse(boxLine[0]),
                    int.Parse(boxLine[1]),
                    int.Parse(boxLine[2])
                )
            });
        }
        
        boxes.Sort((a, b) => {
            int maxDimCompare = b.MaxDim.CompareTo(a.MaxDim);
            if (maxDimCompare != 0) return maxDimCompare;
            return b.Volume.CompareTo(a.Volume);
        });

        var packedBoxes = new List<PackedBox>();
        var skyline = new int[containerW, containerD];

        foreach (var box in boxes)
        {
            PackedBox? bestPlacement = null;
            int minHeight = int.MaxValue;

            foreach (var rotation in box.GetRotations().Distinct())
            {
                if(rotation.W > containerW || rotation.H > containerH || rotation.D > containerD) continue;

                for (int z = 0; z <= containerD - rotation.D; z++)
                {
                    for (int x = 0; x <= containerW - rotation.W; x++)
                    {
                        int currentHeight = 0;
                        for (int dz = 0; dz < rotation.D; dz++)
                        {
                            for (int dx = 0; dx < rotation.W; dx++)
                            {
                                currentHeight = Math.Max(currentHeight, skyline[x + dx, z + dz]);
                            }
                        }

                        if (currentHeight + rotation.H <= containerH)
                        {
                            if (currentHeight < minHeight)
                            {
                                minHeight = currentHeight;
                                bestPlacement = new PackedBox(box.Id, x, currentHeight, z, rotation);
                            }
                        }
                    }
                }
            }

            if (bestPlacement.HasValue)
            {
                var placed = bestPlacement.Value;
                packedBoxes.Add(placed);
                for (int z = 0; z < placed.Dim.D; z++)
                {
                    for (int x = 0; x < placed.Dim.W; x++)
                    {
                        skyline[placed.X + x, placed.Z + z] = placed.Y + placed.Dim.H;
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        Console.WriteLine(packedBoxes.Count);
        foreach (var pbox in packedBoxes)
        {
            Console.WriteLine($"{pbox.Id} {pbox.X} {pbox.Y} {pbox.Z}");
        }
    }
}

Test 18: Minimum Cut Problem (C++) Score: 4.00 / 9
Typical Prompt:
You are writing a C++ program to solve the Minimum Edge Cut problem.

**Target environment specs**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler information**
MSVC cl 19.50.35728 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

You must write a C++ solver that can handle ANY graph size from trivial to multi-gb.

**The Challenge:**
Your C++ program will be tested with graphs ranging from small toy examples to massive real-world networks.
The same program must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Problem:**
Given an undirected connected graph, find the minimum number of edges to remove
such that the graph becomes disconnected (split into two non-empty components).

**Input format (stdin):**
- Line 1: N M (number of nodes, number of edges)
- Lines 2 to M+1: u v (undirected edge between nodes u and v, 0-indexed)

**Output format (stdout):**
- Line 1: K (number of edges in the minimum cut)
- Lines 2 to K+1: u v (each edge in the cut, in any order)

**Requirements:**
1. Program must compile with this compiler, and run on this environment.
2. Read from stdin, write to stdout
3. Handle graphs even if they don't fit in memory.
4. Complete as fast as possible.
5. Output must be a valid cut that disconnects the graph
6. Minimize the number of cut edges
7. Must handle varying graph sizes efficiently

**Example:**
Input:
```
4 5
0 1
1 2
2 3
3 0
0 2
```

Output (one possible answer):
```
2
0 1
3 0
```

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35728 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Be sure that any deviation from the C++ standard library is supported by the given compiler,
as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different algorithms based on graph scale.

Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Find the smallest set of edges to remove from a graph so that it splits into two disconnected pieces. Think of it like finding the weakest point in a network — the fewest cables you’d need to cut to divide it in two.

Subpasses increase the graph size from small networks to very large ones. The AI must implement an efficient algorithm; naïve approaches are far too slow on the bigger inputs.

 1.0000
[6 nodes, 10 edges, min cut ~2] Cut size: 1, Known min: 2, Time: 0.07s - optimal

Subpass 0: 6 nodes, 10 edges, min cut ~2

Score: 1.00

Details: [6 nodes, 10 edges, min cut ~2] Cut size: 1, Known min: 2, Time: 0.07s - optimal

C++ Code 
#include <iostream>
#include <vector>
#include <numeric>
#include <algorithm>
#include <random>
#include <chrono>
#include <cmath>

// Fast I/O
void setup_io() {
    std::ios_base::sync_with_stdio(false);
    std::cin.tie(NULL);
}

// Edge structure
struct Edge {
    int u, v;
};

// Disjoint Set Union (DSU) with path compression and union by size
struct DSU {
    std::vector<int> parent;
    std::vector<int> sz;
    DSU(int n) {
        parent.resize(n);
        std::iota(parent.begin(), parent.end(), 0);
        sz.assign(n, 1);
    }

    int find(int i) {
        if (parent[i] == i)
            return i;
        return parent[i] = find(parent[i]);
    }

    void unite(int i, int j) {
        int root_i = find(i);
        int root_j = find(j);
        if (root_i != root_j) {
            if (sz[root_i] < sz[root_j])
                std::swap(root_i, root_j);
            parent[root_j] = root_i;
            sz[root_i] += sz[root_j];
        }
    }
};

int main() {
    setup_io();

    int n, m;
    std::cin >> n >> m;

    std::vector<Edge> all_edges(m);
    for (int i = 0; i < m; ++i) {
        std::cin >> all_edges[i].u >> all_edges[i].v;
    }

    if (n <= 1) {
        std::cout << 0 << std::endl;
        return 0;
    }

    int min_cut_size = -1;
    std::vector<Edge> min_cut_edges;

    // Seed for random number generator
    auto seed = std::chrono::high_resolution_clock::now().time_since_epoch().count();
    std::mt19937 rng(seed);

    // Adaptive number of trials for Karger's algorithm
    int trials;
    if (n <= 200) {
        // For small N, N^2 is feasible and gives high probability
        trials = n * n; 
    } else {
        // For larger N, use a heuristic that grows slower than N^2
        // C * log(N) is a common choice. We use a larger constant.
        trials = static_cast<int>(20 * std::log(n));
    }
    // A reasonable minimum and maximum for trials
    trials = std::max(10, std::min(trials, 200));

    std::vector<int> edge_indices(m);
    std::iota(edge_indices.begin(), edge_indices.end(), 0);

    for (int i = 0; i < trials; ++i) {
        DSU dsu(n);
        int vertices = n;
        
        std::shuffle(edge_indices.begin(), edge_indices.end(), rng);

        for (int edge_idx : edge_indices) {
            if (vertices <= 2) {
                break;
            }
            const auto& edge = all_edges[edge_idx];
            if (dsu.find(edge.u) != dsu.find(edge.v)) {
                dsu.unite(edge.u, edge.v);
                vertices--;
            }
        }

        int current_cut_size = 0;
        std::vector<Edge> current_cut_edges;
        for (const auto& edge : all_edges) {
            if (dsu.find(edge.u) != dsu.find(edge.v)) {
                current_cut_size++;
                current_cut_edges.push_back(edge);
            }
        }

        if (min_cut_size == -1 || current_cut_size < min_cut_size) {
            min_cut_size = current_cut_size;
            min_cut_edges = current_cut_edges;
        }
    }

    std::cout << min_cut_size << std::endl;
    for (const auto& edge : min_cut_edges) {
        std::cout << edge.u << " " << edge.v << std::endl;
    }

    return 0;
}

void setup_io() {
    std::ios_base::sync_with_stdio(false);
    std::cin.tie(NULL);
}

// Edge structure
struct Edge {
    int u, v;
};

// Disjoint Set Union (DSU) with path compression and union by size
struct DSU {
    std::vector<int> parent;
    std::vector<int> sz;
    DSU(int n) {
        parent.resize(n);
        std::iota(parent.begin(), parent.end(), 0);
        sz.assign(n, 1);
    }

    int find(int i) {
        if (parent[i] == i)
            return i;
        return parent[i] = find(parent[i]);
    }

    void unite(int i, int j) {
        int root_i = find(i);
        int root_j = find(j);
        if (root_i != root_j) {
            if (sz[root_i] < sz[root_j])
                std::swap(root_i, root_j);
            parent[root_j] = root_i;
            sz[root_i] += sz[root_j];
        }
    }
};

    setup_io();

    int n, m;
    std::cin >> n >> m;

    } else {
        // For larger N, use a heuristic that grows slower than N^2
        // C * log(N) is a common choice. We use a larger constant.
        trials = static_cast<int>(20 * std::log(n));
    }
    // A reasonable minimum and maximum for trials
    trials = std::max(10, std::min(trials, 200));

    std::vector<int> edge_indices(m);
    std::iota(edge_indices.begin(), edge_indices.end(), 0);

    for (int i = 0; i < trials; ++i) {

        int vertices = n;

        for (int edge_idx : edge_indices) {
            if (vertices <= 2) {
                break;
            }
            const auto& edge = all_edges[edge_idx];
            if (dsu.find(edge.u) != dsu.find(edge.v)) {
                dsu.unite(edge.u, edge.v);
                vertices--;
            }
        }

        int current_cut_size = 0;
        std::vector<Edge> current_cut_edges;
        for (const auto& edge : all_edges) {
            if (dsu.find(edge.u) != dsu.find(edge.v)) {
                current_cut_size++;
                current_cut_edges.push_back(edge);
            }
        }

        if (min_cut_size == -1 || current_cut_size < min_cut_size) {
            min_cut_size = current_cut_size;
            min_cut_edges = current_cut_edges;
        }
    }

    std::cout << min_cut_size << std::endl;
    for (const auto& edge : min_cut_edges) {
        std::cout << edge.u << " " << edge.v << std::endl;
    }

    return 0;
}

}

// Edge structure

};

// Disjoint Set Union (DSU) with path compression and union by size

    std::vector<int> parent;
    std::vector<int> sz;

        parent.resize(n);
        std::iota(parent.begin(), parent.end(), 0);

#include <numeric>

// Fast I/O
void setup_io() {
    std::ios_base::sync_with_stdio(false);
    std::cin.tie(NULL);
}

// Edge structure
struct Edge {
    int u, v;
};

// Disjoint Set Union (DSU) with path compression and union by size
struct DSU {
    std::vector<int> parent;
    std::vector<int> sz;
    DSU(int n) {
        parent.resize(n);
        std::iota(parent.begin(), parent.end(), 0);
        sz.assign(n, 1);
    }

    int find(int i) {
        if (parent[i] == i)
            return i;
        return parent[i] = find(parent[i]);
    }

    void unite(int i, int j) {
        int root_i = find(i);
        int root_j = find(j);
        if (root_i != root_j) {
            if (sz[root_i] < sz[root_j])
                std::swap(root_i, root_j);
            parent[root_j] = root_i;
            sz[root_i] += sz[root_j];
        }
    }
};

int main() {
    setup_io();

    int n, m;
    std::cin >> n >> m;

    std::vector<Edge> all_edges(m);
    for (int i = 0; i < m; ++i) {
        std::cin >> all_edges[i].u >> all_edges[i].v;
    }

        std::cout << 0 << std::endl;
        return 0;
    }

    int min_cut_size = -1;
    std::vector<Edge> min_cut_edges;

    // Seed for random number generator
    auto seed = std::chrono::high_resolution_clock::now().time_since_epoch().count();
    std::mt19937 rng(seed);

    // Adaptive number of trials for Karger's algorithm
    int trials;
    if (n <= 200) {
        // For small N, N^2 is feasible and gives high probability
        trials = n * n; 
    } else {
        // For larger N, use a heuristic that grows slower than N^2
        // C * log(N) is a common choice. We use a larger constant.
        trials = static_cast<int>(20 * std::log(n));
    }
    // A reasonable minimum and maximum for trials
    trials = std::max(10, std::min(trials, 200));

    std::vector<int> edge_indices(m);
    std::iota(edge_indices.begin(), edge_indices.end(), 0);

    for (int i = 0; i < trials; ++i) {
        DSU dsu(n);
        int vertices = n;
        
        std::shuffle(edge_indices.begin(), edge_indices.end(), rng);

        for (int edge_idx : edge_indices) {
            if (vertices <= 2) {
                break;
            }
            const auto& edge = all_edges[edge_idx];
            if (dsu.find(edge.u) != dsu.find(edge.v)) {
                dsu.unite(edge.u, edge.v);
                vertices--;
            }
        }

        int current_cut_size = 0;
        std::vector<Edge> current_cut_edges;
        for (const auto& edge : all_edges) {
            if (dsu.find(edge.u) != dsu.find(edge.v)) {
                current_cut_size++;
                current_cut_edges.push_back(edge);
            }
        }

        if (min_cut_size == -1 || current_cut_size < min_cut_size) {
            min_cut_size = current_cut_size;
            min_cut_edges = current_cut_edges;
        }
    }

    std::cout << min_cut_size << std::endl;
    for (const auto& edge : min_cut_edges) {
        std::cout << edge.u << " " << edge.v << std::endl;
    }

    return 0;

C++ Code 
#include <iostream>
#include <vector>
#include <numeric>
#include <algorithm>
#include <random>
#include <chrono>
#include <cmath>

// Fast I/O
void setup_io() {
    std::ios_base::sync_with_stdio(false);
    std::cin.tie(NULL);
}

// Edge structure
struct Edge {
    int u, v;
};

// Disjoint Set Union (DSU) with path compression and union by size
struct DSU {
    std::vector<int> parent;
    std::vector<int> sz;
    DSU(int n) {
        parent.resize(n);
        std::iota(parent.begin(), parent.end(), 0);
        sz.assign(n, 1);
    }

    int find(int i) {
        if (parent[i] == i)
            return i;
        return parent[i] = find(parent[i]);
    }

    void unite(int i, int j) {
        int root_i = find(i);
        int root_j = find(j);
        if (root_i != root_j) {
            if (sz[root_i] < sz[root_j])
                std::swap(root_i, root_j);
            parent[root_j] = root_i;
            sz[root_i] += sz[root_j];
        }
    }
};

int main() {
    setup_io();

    int n, m;
    std::cin >> n >> m;

    std::vector<Edge> all_edges(m);
    for (int i = 0; i < m; ++i) {
        std::cin >> all_edges[i].u >> all_edges[i].v;
    }

    if (n <= 1) {
        std::cout << 0 << std::endl;
        return 0;
    }

    int min_cut_size = -1;
    std::vector<Edge> min_cut_edges;

    // Seed for random number generator
    auto seed = std::chrono::high_resolution_clock::now().time_since_epoch().count();
    std::mt19937 rng(seed);

    // Adaptive number of trials for Karger's algorithm
    int trials;
    if (n <= 200) {
        // For small N, N^2 is feasible and gives high probability
        trials = n * n; 
    } else {
        // For larger N, use a heuristic that grows slower than N^2
        // C * log(N) is a common choice. We use a larger constant.
        trials = static_cast<int>(20 * std::log(n));
    }
    // A reasonable minimum and maximum for trials
    trials = std::max(10, std::min(trials, 200));

    std::vector<int> edge_indices(m);
    std::iota(edge_indices.begin(), edge_indices.end(), 0);

    for (int i = 0; i < trials; ++i) {
        DSU dsu(n);
        int vertices = n;
        
        std::shuffle(edge_indices.begin(), edge_indices.end(), rng);

        for (int edge_idx : edge_indices) {
            if (vertices <= 2) {
                break;
            }
            const auto& edge = all_edges[edge_idx];
            if (dsu.find(edge.u) != dsu.find(edge.v)) {
                dsu.unite(edge.u, edge.v);
                vertices--;
            }
        }

        int current_cut_size = 0;
        std::vector<Edge> current_cut_edges;
        for (const auto& edge : all_edges) {
            if (dsu.find(edge.u) != dsu.find(edge.v)) {
                current_cut_size++;
                current_cut_edges.push_back(edge);
            }
        }

        if (min_cut_size == -1 || current_cut_size < min_cut_size) {
            min_cut_size = current_cut_size;
            min_cut_edges = current_cut_edges;
        }
    }

    std::cout << min_cut_size << std::endl;
    for (const auto& edge : min_cut_edges) {
        std::cout << edge.u << " " << edge.v << std::endl;
    }

    return 0;
}

Test 19: 3D Voxel Mining (C#) Score: 0.00 / 13
Typical Prompt:
You are writing a C# program to solve a mining problem in a simplified 3D voxel space.

**Constraints:**
1. Can only dig dirt that is exposed from above or a side.
2. Dug dirt must be dumped somewhere - at a location not blocking future digging
3. Maximum uphill slope - 1 Z voxel per X/Y voxel.
4. Dumped dirt becomes a new obstacle (plan carefully!)
5. Goal is to get valuable ore to a processing plant located at x=0, y=0
6. Ground is stable and solid, and you can dig under any voxel without fear of it collapsing
so long as it's connected to the earth. If you disconnect a voxel(s), it will collapse.

The voxel grid size has 10x more precision in Z than X/Y. So 10x10x100 is a cube.

**Input header format (stdin - space delimited. Ends with newline):**
```
XVoxelCount YVoxelCount ZVoxelCount XBlockSize YBlockSize ZBlockSize
```
Counts are human readable integers. BlockSize are human readable decimal numbers.

After the newline, voxels then follow in bytes, like this python psuedocode:
```
for x in range(XVoxelCount):
for y in range(YVoxelCount):
for z in range(ZVoxelCount):
d = readByte(stdin)
massInTonnes = d % 16
valuePerTonne = d // 16
addVoxel(d)
```

Each peice of dirt needs to be moved to its new location - you need to trace the
path in X and Y (4-connected / manhatten steps) and ensure that the path doesn't climb uphill
or downhill more than 1 Z voxel for every X/Y voxel.

**Output format (stdout / newline seperated):**
```
M (number of operations)
N digX digY digZ stepX1 stepY1 stepX2 stepY2 (N steps) destX destY (for each operation)
```

If dumping valuable ore at the processing plant, the voxel ceases to exist. If a voxel is
dumped elsewhere, it is dumped on top of the ground, raising the ground by 1 voxel. It can
be dug again and moved later if required.

Every unit of mass requires $1 of fuel to move one voxel in X or Y when flat, or $5 of fuel
to go up a 10% slope. Downhill is free.

Getting valuable ore to your processing plant pays $1000 per unit of mass per unit of value.
The most valuable ore (#15) pays $15,000 per ton, so the most a single dump truck load can gross
is $225,000.

(Dumping junk ore at the processing plant causes damage to the plant, costing $1000 per ton.)

**Requirements:**
1. Program must compile with .NET/csc
2. Read from stdin, write to stdout
3. World size could be anything from 10x10x10 to 5000x5000x5000, your code should handle both extremes.
4. Your code should complete in under 5 minutes
5. Maximize profit.

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler:**
C# (.NET SDK 10.0.201; SDKs 10.0.201) | csc 5.3.0-2.26153.122 (4d3023de605a78ba3e59e50c657eed70f125c68a) | max LangVersion 14


Write complete, compilable C# code with a Main method.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on problem scale.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Plan an open-pit mine in a 3D block model. Each block has a value (ore worth money, waste costs money to move) and you can only dig a block once the blocks above it have been removed. The goal is to maximise profit by choosing which blocks to extract and where to dump the waste.

The AI must balance revenue against haulage costs, respect slope constraints for road access, and avoid dumping waste where it blocks future digging. Subpasses increase the pit size and complexity.

 0.0000

Determining projects to restore...
Restored D:\CodingBenchmark\compile_cache\gemini-2_5-pro-LowReasoning\csharp\project_15be2b55e4dd9c39\Program.csproj (in 80 ms).

3D Voxel Mining - 10×10 tiny pit (10 KB)

View C# Code (15264 chars)
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Diagnostics;
using System.Globalization;
using System.IO;
using System.Linq;
using System.Text;

public class Voxel
{
    public byte RawData { get; }
    public int Mass => RawData & 0x0F;
    public int Value => (RawData >> 4) & 0x0F;
    public bool IsOre => Value > 0;
    public Voxel(byte data) { RawData = data; }
}

public class Operation
{
    public (int x, int y, int z) DigPos { get; set; }
    public (int x, int y) DestPos { get; set; }
    public List<(int x, int y)> Path { get; set; }
    public double Cost { get; set; }

    public override string ToString()
    {
        var sb = new StringBuilder();
        sb.Append($"{Path.Count} {DigPos.x} {DigPos.y} {DigPos.z}");
        if (Path.Count > 0)
        {
            sb.Append(" ");
            sb.Append(string.Join(" ", Path.Select(p => $"{p.x} {p.y}")));
        }
        sb.Append($" {DestPos.x} {DestPos.y}");
        return sb.ToString();
    }
}

public class World
{
    private readonly Dictionary<(int x, int y, int z), Voxel> _voxels = new Dictionary<(int, int, int), Voxel>();
    private readonly int[] _surfaceHeight;

    public int XCount { get; }
    public int YCount { get; }
    public int ZCount { get; }

    public World(int xc, int yc, int zc)
    {
        XCount = xc; YCount = yc; ZCount = zc;
        if ((long)xc * yc < 25_000_000) // Avoid huge allocation for very large maps
        {
            _surfaceHeight = new int[xc * yc];
        }
        else
        {
            _surfaceHeight = new int[1]; // Dummy array if world is too large for surface cache
        }
    }

    public Voxel GetVoxel(int x, int y, int z)
    {
        if (x < 0 || x >= XCount || y < 0 || y >= YCount || z < 0 || z >= ZCount) return null;
        _voxels.TryGetValue((x, y, z), out var v);
        return v;
    }

    public void SetVoxel(int x, int y, int z, Voxel v)
    {
        if (x < 0 || x >= XCount || y < 0 || y >= YCount || z < 0 || z >= ZCount) return;

        bool changed = false;
        if (v == null || v.Mass == 0)
        {
            if (_voxels.Remove((x, y, z)))
                changed = true;
        }
        else
        {
            _voxels[(x, y, z)] = v;
            changed = true;
        }

        if (changed && IsUsingSurfaceCache())
        {
            UpdateSurfaceCache(x, y);
        }
    }

    public int GetSurfaceZ(int x, int y)
    {
        if (x < 0 || x >= XCount || y < 0 || y >= YCount) return 0;
        if (!IsUsingSurfaceCache()) return FindSurfaceZ(x, y);
        return _surfaceHeight[y * XCount + x];
    }
    
    private bool IsUsingSurfaceCache() => _surfaceHeight.Length > 1;

    public void InitializeSurface()
    {
        if (!IsUsingSurfaceCache()) return;
        for (int x = 0; x < XCount; x++)
        {
            for (int y = 0; y < YCount; y++)
            {
                _surfaceHeight[y * XCount + x] = FindSurfaceZ(x, y);
            }
        }
    }

    private void UpdateSurfaceCache(int x, int y)
    {
        _surfaceHeight[y * XCount + x] = FindSurfaceZ(x, y);
    }

    private int FindSurfaceZ(int x, int y)
    {
        for (int z = ZCount - 1; z >= 0; z--)
        {
            if (GetVoxel(x, y, z) != null) return z + 1;
        }
        return 0;
    }

    public IEnumerable<KeyValuePair<(int, int, int), Voxel>> GetAllVoxels() => _voxels;
}

public class Pathfinder
{
    private class PathNode
    {
        public (int x, int y, int z) Pos { get; set; }
        public double GCost { get; set; }
        public double HCost { get; set; }
        public double FCost => GCost + HCost;
        public PathNode Parent { get; set; }
    }

    private readonly World _world;
    public Pathfinder(World world) { _world = world; }

    public (List<(int x, int y)> path, double cost) FindPath(int startX, int startY, int destX, int destY, int mass)
    {
        int startZ = _world.GetSurfaceZ(startX, startY);

        var startNode = new PathNode { Pos = (startX, startY, startZ), GCost = 0 };
        startNode.HCost = Heuristic(startX, startY, destX, destY, mass);

        var openSet = new PriorityQueue<PathNode, double>();
        openSet.Enqueue(startNode, startNode.FCost);
        var allNodes = new Dictionary<(int, int), PathNode> { { (startX, startY), startNode } };

        while (openSet.Count > 0)
        {
            var currentNode = openSet.Dequeue();
            if (currentNode.Pos.x == destX && currentNode.Pos.y == destY)
            {
                return (ReconstructPath(currentNode), currentNode.GCost);
            }

            int[][] moves = { new[] { 0, 1 }, new[] { 0, -1 }, new[] { 1, 0 }, new[] { -1, 0 } };
            foreach (var move in moves)
            {
                int nextX = currentNode.Pos.x + move[0];
                int nextY = currentNode.Pos.y + move[1];

                if (nextX < 0 || nextX >= _world.XCount || nextY < 0 || nextY >= _world.YCount) continue;

                int currentZ = currentNode.Pos.z;
                int nextZ = _world.GetSurfaceZ(nextX, nextY);

                if (nextZ - currentZ > 1) continue;

                double moveCost;
                if (nextZ > currentZ) moveCost = 5.0 * mass;
                else if (nextZ < currentZ) moveCost = 0;
                else moveCost = 1.0 * mass;
                
                double newGCost = currentNode.GCost + moveCost;

                if (!allNodes.TryGetValue((nextX, nextY), out var nextNode) || newGCost < nextNode.GCost)
                {
                    if (nextNode == null)
                    {
                        nextNode = new PathNode { Pos = (nextX, nextY, nextZ) };
                        allNodes[(nextX, nextY)] = nextNode;
                    }

                    nextNode.Parent = currentNode;
                    nextNode.GCost = newGCost;
                    nextNode.HCost = Heuristic(nextX, nextY, destX, destY, mass);
                    openSet.Enqueue(nextNode, nextNode.FCost);
                }
            }
        }
        return (null, -1);
    }

    private double Heuristic(int x, int y, int destX, int destY, int mass) => (Math.Abs(x - destX) + Math.Abs(y - destY)) * mass;

    private List<(int x, int y)> ReconstructPath(PathNode node)
    {
        var path = new List<(int x, int y)>();
        var current = node;
        while (current.Parent != null)
        {
            path.Add((current.Pos.x, current.Pos.y));
            current = current.Parent;
        }
        path.Reverse();
        return path;
    }
}

public class Program
{
    private static World _world;
    private static Pathfinder _pathfinder;
    private static readonly Stopwatch _stopwatch = new Stopwatch();
    private static HashSet<(int, int, int)> _diggableCache;

    private class ProfitableOperation
    {
        public Operation Op { get; set; }
        public double Profit { get; set; }
    }

    public static void Main(string[] args)
    {
        _stopwatch.Start();
        CultureInfo.CurrentCulture = CultureInfo.InvariantCulture;

        ReadInput();
        _pathfinder = new Pathfinder(_world);
        _diggableCache = FindAllDiggableVoxels();
        
        var operations = new List<Operation>();

        while (_stopwatch.Elapsed.TotalMinutes < 4.8)
        {
            var bestOp = FindBestProfitableOperation();
            if (bestOp == null || bestOp.Profit <= 0) break;
            
            ExecuteOperation(bestOp.Op);
            operations.Add(bestOp.Op);
        }

        PrintOutput(operations);
    }

    private static void ReadInput()
    {
        string header = Console.ReadLine();
        string[] parts = header.Split(' ');
        int xCount = int.Parse(parts[0]);
        int yCount = int.Parse(parts[1]);
        int zCount = int.Parse(parts[2]);

        _world = new World(xCount, yCount, zCount);
        
        using (var stream = Console.OpenStandardInput())
        {
            byte[] buffer = new byte[1024 * 1024];
            int bytesRead;
            long totalBytesRead = 0;
            long totalBytesExpected = (long)xCount * yCount * zCount;

            while (totalBytesRead < totalBytesExpected && (bytesRead = stream.Read(buffer, 0, buffer.Length)) > 0)
            {
                for (int i = 0; i < bytesRead; i++)
                {
                    long currentByteIndex = totalBytesRead + i;
                    int z = (int)(currentByteIndex % zCount);
                    int y = (int)((currentByteIndex / zCount) % yCount);
                    int x = (int)(currentByteIndex / (yCount * zCount));
                    
                    var voxel = new Voxel(buffer[i]);
                    if (voxel.Mass > 0)
                    {
                        _world.SetVoxel(x, y, z, voxel);
                    }
                }
                totalBytesRead += bytesRead;
            }
        }
        _world.InitializeSurface();
    }
    
    private static bool IsExposed(int x, int y, int z)
    {
        return _world.GetVoxel(x + 1, y, z) == null || _world.GetVoxel(x - 1, y, z) == null ||
               _world.GetVoxel(x, y + 1, z) == null || _world.GetVoxel(x, y - 1, z) == null ||
               _world.GetVoxel(x, y, z + 1) == null || _world.GetVoxel(x, y, z - 1) == null;
    }

    private static HashSet<(int, int, int)> FindAllDiggableVoxels()
    {
        var diggable = new HashSet<(int, int, int)>();
        foreach (var kvp in _world.GetAllVoxels())
        {
            if (IsExposed(kvp.Key.Item1, kvp.Key.Item2, kvp.Key.Item3))
            {
                diggable.Add(kvp.Key);
            }
        }
        return diggable;
    }

    private static ProfitableOperation FindBestProfitableOperation()
    {
        ProfitableOperation bestOp = null;

        var oreVoxels = _diggableCache.ToList().Where(p => _world.GetVoxel(p.x, p.y, p.z)?.IsOre ?? false);

        foreach (var pos in oreVoxels)
        {
            var voxel = _world.GetVoxel(pos.x, pos.y, pos.z);
            if (voxel == null) continue;

            double revenue = voxel.Value * voxel.Mass * 1000.0;
            
            var neighbors = new[] { (pos.x + 1, pos.y), (pos.x - 1, pos.y), (pos.x, pos.y + 1), (pos.x, pos.y - 1) };
            foreach (var startPos in neighbors)
            {
                if (_world.GetVoxel(startPos.Item1, pos.y, pos.z) != null) continue;

                var (path, cost) = _pathfinder.FindPath(startPos.Item1, startPos.Item2, 0, 0, voxel.Mass);
                if (path != null)
                {
                    double profit = revenue - cost;
                    if (bestOp == null || profit > bestOp.Profit)
                    {
                        bestOp = new ProfitableOperation
                        {
                            Op = new Operation { DigPos = pos, DestPos = (0, 0), Path = path, Cost = cost },
                            Profit = profit
                        };
                    }
                }
            }
        }
        return bestOp;
    }

    private static void ExecuteOperation(Operation op)
    {
        var pos = op.DigPos;
        _world.SetVoxel(pos.x, pos.y, pos.z, null);
        
        UpdateDiggableCache(pos.x, pos.y, pos.z, isDig: true);

        CheckAndHandleCollapses(pos.x, pos.y, pos.z);
    }

    private static void CheckAndHandleCollapses(int x, int y, int z)
    {
        var neighbors = new[]
        {
            (x + 1, y, z), (x - 1, y, z),
            (x, y + 1, z), (x, y - 1, z),
            (x, y, z + 1), (x, y, z - 1)
        };

        foreach (var n in neighbors)
        {
            if (_world.GetVoxel(n.Item1, n.Item2, n.Item3) != null && !IsSupported(n.Item1, n.Item2, n.Item3))
            {
                var component = FindUnsupportedComponent(n.Item1, n.Item2, n.Item3);
                foreach (var block in component)
                {
                    if (_world.GetVoxel(block.x, block.y, block.z) != null)
                    {
                        _world.SetVoxel(block.x, block.y, block.z, null);
                        UpdateDiggableCache(block.x, block.y, block.z, isDig: true);
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }

    private static bool IsSupported(int x, int y, int z)
    {
        var q = new Queue<(int, int, int)>();
        var visited = new HashSet<(int, int, int)>();

        q.Enqueue((x, y, z));
        visited.Add((x, y, z));

        while (q.Count > 0)
        {
            var curr = q.Dequeue();
            if (curr.Item3 == 0) return true;

            var neighbors = new[]
            {
                (curr.Item1 + 1, curr.Item2, curr.Item3), (curr.Item1 - 1, curr.Item2, curr.Item3),
                (curr.Item1, curr.Item2 + 1, curr.Item3), (curr.Item1, curr.Item2 - 1, curr.Item3),
                (curr.Item1, curr.Item2, curr.Item3 + 1), (curr.Item1, curr.Item2, curr.Item3 - 1)
            };

            foreach (var n in neighbors)
            {
                if (!visited.Contains(n) && _world.GetVoxel(n.Item1, n.Item2, n.Item3) != null)
                {
                    visited.Add(n);
                    q.Enqueue(n);
                }
            }
        }
        return false;
    }

    private static HashSet<(int x, int y, int z)> FindUnsupportedComponent(int x, int y, int z)
    {
        var component = new HashSet<(int x, int y, int z)>();
        var q = new Queue<(int, int, int)>();

        if (_world.GetVoxel(x, y, z) == null) return component;

        q.Enqueue((x, y, z));
        component.Add((x, y, z));

        while (q.Count > 0)
        {
            var curr = q.Dequeue();
            var neighbors = new[]
            {
                (curr.Item1 + 1, curr.Item2, curr.Item3), (curr.Item1 - 1, curr.Item2, curr.Item3),
                (curr.Item1, curr.Item2 + 1, curr.Item3), (curr.Item1, curr.Item2 - 1, curr.Item3),
                (curr.Item1, curr.Item2, curr.Item3 + 1), (curr.Item1, curr.Item2, curr.Item3 - 1)
            };

            foreach (var n in neighbors)
            {
                if (!component.Contains(n) && _world.GetVoxel(n.Item1, n.Item2, n.Item3) != null)
                {
                    component.Add(n);
                    q.Enqueue(n);
                }
            }
        }
        return component;
    }
    
    private static void UpdateDiggableCache(int x, int y, int z, bool isDig)
    {
        if (isDig) _diggableCache.Remove((x, y, z));
        
        var neighbors = new[]
        {
            (x+1,y,z), (x-1,y,z), (x,y+1,z), (x,y-1,z), (x,y,z+1), (x,y,z-1)
        };

        foreach(var n in neighbors)
        {
            var neighborVoxel = _world.GetVoxel(n.Item1, n.Item2, n.Item3);
            if (neighborVoxel != null)
            {
                if (IsExposed(n.Item1, n.Item2, n.Item3))
                    _diggableCache.Add(n);
                else
                    _diggableCache.Remove(n);
            }
        }
    }

    private static void PrintOutput(List<Operation> operations)
    {
        Console.WriteLine(operations.Count);
        foreach (var op in operations)
        {
            Console.WriteLine(op.ToString());
        }
    }
}

Test 20: Drillhole Data Validation (Rust) Score: 0.00 / 14
Typical Prompt:
You are writing a Rust program to validate drillhole assay data.


**The Challenge:**
Your Rust program will be tested with drillhole datasets ranging from small exploration projects to
massive mining surveys. The same program must work efficiently across ALL scales from 10 holes to
200,000 holes.

**Problem:**
Given drillhole data from a mining exploration project, identify samples that likely contain typos or
measurement errors. These outliers don't correlate well with spatially neighboring data points.

**Target environment specs**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**Rust Compiler information**
rustc 1.92.0 (ded5c06cf 2025-12-08) | edition=2024 | host: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc

**Drillhole Data:**
- Each hole has a 3D path: start point (x,y,z) + direction vector + length
- Samples are taken at intervals along each hole
- Each sample has measurements for multiple properties

**Input format (stdin):**
```
num_holes num_properties
property_name_1 property_name_2 ...
hole_id start_x start_y start_z dir_x dir_y dir_z length num_samples
depth prop1 prop2 ...
depth prop1 prop2 ...
etc.
hole_id start_x start_y start_z dir_x dir_y dir_z length num_samples
```

**Output format (stdout):**
```
M (number of suspect entries found)
hole_id sample_idx property_name confidence (for each suspect, sorted by confidence desc)
```

**Requirements:**
1. Program must compile with rustc and only the standard rust library.
2. Read from stdin, write to stdout
3. Handle up to thousands of holes with thousands of samples each
4. Complete within 5 minutes
5. Report at least the most obvious outliers
6. Confidence score should reflect how anomalous the value is
7. Must handle varying dataset sizes efficiently

Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main function.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on dataset scale.

The program should be robust enough to handle edge cases like missing data, duplicate entries, and varying data distributions.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)

Given a set of mining drillholes — each a 3D path with measurements taken at intervals along its length — identify the data entries that are most likely to be errors (typos, instrument glitches, or transcription mistakes).

The AI must compare each measurement against its spatial neighbours: a reading that disagrees with nearby drillholes is suspect. Subpasses increase the number of drillholes and properties, requiring efficient spatial reasoning at scale.

Test 21: N-Dimensional Snake Game (C++) Score: 5.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C++ code to play an N-Dimensional Snake Game.

You must write a C++ solver that can handle ANY dimensional complexity from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: 2D games (eg 8x8), few obstacles, basic pathfinding
- **Medium**: 3D-4D games (eg 6x6x6 to 8x8x8x8), moderate obstacles, intermediate planning
- **Large**: 6D-8D games (eg 4x4x4x4x4x4 to 6x6x6x6x6x6x6x6), many obstacles, complex pathfinding
- **Extreme**: 10D-20D games (eg 3x3x3... to 4x4x4...)

**The Challenge:**
Your C++ snake player will be tested with games ranging from simple 2D boards to massive 20D hypercubes.
The same algorithm must work efficiently across ALL dimensionalities.

**Problem:**
Like classic Nokia snake, but generalized to arbitrary dimensions. The snake occupies a series of
connected integer grid points in N-D space, moves one step per turn along one axis (+1 or -1),
grows when eating food, and dies if it hits an obstacle or itself.

**Input format (stdin, whitespace / newline seperated):**
```
D max_turns (D = number of dimensions, max_turns = turn limit)
bounds (D integers: size of space in each dimension)
snake_length start_pos (length, then D integers for head position)
F (number of food items)
F lines: food positions (D integers each)
O (number of obstacles)
O lines: obstacle positions (D integers each)
```

**Output format (stdout):**
Each turn, output: axis direction
- axis: 0 to D-1 (which dimension to move along)
- direction: -1 or +1 (which way to move)

**Game ends when:**
- Snake eats all food (win)
- Snake hits obstacle or itself (lose)
- Max turns reached

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35728 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Be sure that any deviation from the C++ standard library is supported by the given compiler,
as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Play the classic Snake game, but in more than two dimensions. The snake moves through an N-dimensional grid, eating food to grow while avoiding its own body and the walls. In higher dimensions the grid is enormous but self-collision becomes far trickier to reason about.

Subpasses increase the number of dimensions, making the state space explode. The AI must write a strategy that plans a safe path to food without boxing itself in.

 1.0000
[2D 20x20, 5 food, 10 obstacles] Food: 5/5, Turns: 31, End: won, Time: 0.07s

N-Dimensional Snake - 2D 20x20, 5 food, 10 obstacles

View C++ Code (8078 chars)
#include <iostream>
#include <vector>
#include <queue>
#include <unordered_set>
#include <unordered_map>
#include <cmath>
#include <algorithm>
#include <list>

using Point = std::vector<int>;

struct PointHash {
    std::size_t operator()(const Point& p) const {
        std::size_t hash = 0;
        for (int i : p) {
            hash ^= std::hash<int>{}(i) + 0x9e3779b9 + (hash << 6) + (hash >> 2);
        }
        return hash;
    }
};

using ObstacleSet = std::unordered_set<Point, PointHash>;

struct AStarNode {
    Point pos;
    int f_cost;

    bool operator>(const AStarNode& other) const {
        return f_cost > other.f_cost;
    }
};

int manhattan_distance(const Point& a, const Point& b) {
    int distance = 0;
    for (size_t i = 0; i < a.size(); ++i) {
        distance += std::abs(a[i] - b[i]);
    }
    return distance;
}

std::list<Point> a_star_search(
    const Point& start,
    const Point& goal,
    int D,
    const std::vector<int>& bounds,
    const ObstacleSet& obstacles) {

    std::priority_queue<AStarNode, std::vector<AStarNode>, std::greater<AStarNode>> open_set;
    std::unordered_map<Point, Point, PointHash> came_from;
    std::unordered_map<Point, int, PointHash> g_score;

    g_score[start] = 0;
    open_set.push({start, manhattan_distance(start, goal)});

    while (!open_set.empty()) {
        Point current = open_set.top().pos;
        open_set.pop();

        if (current == goal) {
            std::list<Point> path;
            while (came_from.count(current)) {
                path.push_front(current);
                current = came_from[current];
            }
            path.push_front(start);
            return path;
        }

        for (int i = 0; i < D; ++i) {
            for (int dir = -1; dir <= 1; dir += 2) {
                Point neighbor = current;
                neighbor[i] += dir;

                bool out_of_bounds = false;
                for(int j = 0; j < D; ++j) {
                    if (neighbor[j] < 0 || neighbor[j] >= bounds[j]) {
                        out_of_bounds = true;
                        break;
                    }
                }
                if (out_of_bounds || obstacles.count(neighbor)) {
                    continue;
                }

                int tentative_g_score = g_score[current] + 1;
                if (!g_score.count(neighbor) || tentative_g_score < g_score[neighbor]) {
                    came_from[neighbor] = current;
                    g_score[neighbor] = tentative_g_score;
                    int f_score = tentative_g_score + manhattan_distance(neighbor, goal);
                    open_set.push({neighbor, f_score});
                }
            }
        }
    }

    return {}; // No path found
}


int main() {
    std::ios_base::sync_with_stdio(false);
    std::cin.tie(NULL);

    int D, max_turns;
    std::cin >> D >> max_turns;

    std::vector<int> bounds(D);
    for (int i = 0; i < D; ++i) std::cin >> bounds[i];

    int snake_length;
    Point start_pos(D);
    std::cin >> snake_length;
    for (int i = 0; i < D; ++i) std::cin >> start_pos[i];

    std::list<Point> snake;
    snake.push_back(start_pos);
    
    int F;
    std::cin >> F;
    std::vector<Point> food(F, Point(D));
    for (int i = 0; i < F; ++i) {
        for (int j = 0; j < D; ++j) {
            std::cin >> food[i][j];
        }
    }

    int O;
    std::cin >> O;
    ObstacleSet permanent_obstacles;
    for (int i = 0; i < O; ++i) {
        Point obs(D);
        for (int j = 0; j < D; ++j) {
            std::cin >> obs[j];
        }
        permanent_obstacles.insert(obs);
    }

    for (int t = 0; t < max_turns; ++t) {
        Point head = snake.front();

        // Sort food by distance
        std::sort(food.begin(), food.end(), [&](const Point& a, const Point& b) {
            return manhattan_distance(head, a) < manhattan_distance(head, b);
        });

        std::list<Point> best_path;
        Point best_food_target;

        ObstacleSet current_obstacles = permanent_obstacles;
        auto it = snake.begin();
        std::advance(it, 1); // Skip head
        for (; it != snake.end(); ++it) {
            current_obstacles.insert(*it);
        }

        for (const auto& food_item : food) {
            std::list<Point> path_to_food = a_star_search(head, food_item, D, bounds, current_obstacles);

            if (!path_to_food.empty()) {
                // Safety check: can we reach our tail after eating?
                ObstacleSet future_obstacles = permanent_obstacles;
                std::list<Point> future_snake = snake;
                
                auto path_it = path_to_food.begin();
                std::advance(path_it, 1);
                for(; path_it != path_to_food.end(); ++path_it) {
                    future_snake.push_front(*path_it);
                }

                while(future_snake.size() > snake.size() + path_to_food.size() - 1) {
                    future_snake.pop_back();
                }
                
                for(const auto& p : future_snake) {
                    future_obstacles.insert(p);
                }
                future_obstacles.erase(future_snake.back());

                std::list<Point> path_to_tail = a_star_search(food_item, future_snake.back(), D, bounds, future_obstacles);
                
                if (!path_to_tail.empty()) {
                    best_path = path_to_food;
                    best_food_target = food_item;
                    break;
                }
            }
        }

        Point next_pos;
        if (!best_path.empty()) {
            auto it = best_path.begin();
            std::advance(it, 1);
            next_pos = *it;
        } else {
            // Survival mode: move to tail
            if (snake.size() > 1) {
                current_obstacles.erase(snake.back());
            }
            std::list<Point> path_to_tail = a_star_search(head, snake.back(), D, bounds, current_obstacles);
            if (!path_to_tail.empty()) {
                 auto it = path_to_tail.begin();
                 std::advance(it, 1);
                 next_pos = *it;
            } else {
                // Desperation: move to any adjacent free square
                bool moved = false;
                for (int i = 0; i < D; ++i) {
                    for (int dir = -1; dir <= 1; dir += 2) {
                        Point neighbor = head;
                        neighbor[i] += dir;

                        bool out_of_bounds = false;
                        for(int j=0; j<D; ++j) {
                             if(neighbor[j] < 0 || neighbor[j] >= bounds[j]) {
                                 out_of_bounds = true;
                                 break;
                             }
                        }

                        if (!out_of_bounds && !current_obstacles.count(neighbor) && !permanent_obstacles.count(neighbor)) {
                            next_pos = neighbor;
                            moved = true;
                            break;
                        }
                    }
                    if(moved) break;
                }
                 if(!moved) { // Trapped
                    next_pos = head; next_pos[0]++;
                 }
            }
        }

        int move_axis = -1, move_dir = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < D; ++i) {
            if (next_pos[i] != head[i]) {
                move_axis = i;
                move_dir = next_pos[i] - head[i];
                break;
            }
        }

        std::cout << move_axis << " " << move_dir << std::endl;

        snake.push_front(next_pos);

        bool ate_food = false;
        if (!food.empty()) {
            auto food_it = std::find(food.begin(), food.end(), next_pos);
            if (food_it != food.end()) {
                ate_food = true;
                food.erase(food_it);
            }
        }

        if (!ate_food) {
            snake.pop_back();
        }

        if (food.empty()) {
           // All food eaten, just keep surviving
        }
    }

    return 0;
}
Subpass 1
Subpass configurationView exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

#include <iostream>
#include <vector>
#include <queue>
#include <unordered_set>
#include <unordered_map>
#include <cmath>
#include <algorithm>
#include <list>

using Point = std::vector<int>;

struct PointHash {
    std::size_t operator()(const Point& p) const {
        std::size_t hash = 0;
        for (int i : p) {
            hash ^= std::hash<int>{}(i) + 0x9e3779b9 + (hash << 6) + (hash >> 2);
        }
        return hash;
    }
};

using ObstacleSet = std::unordered_set<Point, PointHash>;

struct AStarNode {
    Point pos;
    int f_cost;

    bool operator>(const AStarNode& other) const {
        return f_cost > other.f_cost;
    }
};

int manhattan_distance(const Point& a, const Point& b) {
    int distance = 0;
    for (size_t i = 0; i < a.size(); ++i) {
        distance += std::abs(a[i] - b[i]);
    }
    return distance;
}

std::list<Point> a_star_search(
    const Point& start,
    const Point& goal,
    int D,
    const std::vector<int>& bounds,
    const ObstacleSet& obstacles) {

    std::priority_queue<AStarNode, std::vector<AStarNode>, std::greater<AStarNode>> open_set;
    std::unordered_map<Point, Point, PointHash> came_from;
    std::unordered_map<Point, int, PointHash> g_score;

    g_score[start] = 0;
    open_set.push({start, manhattan_distance(start, goal)});

    while (!open_set.empty()) {
        Point current = open_set.top().pos;
        open_set.pop();

        if (current == goal) {
            std::list<Point> path;
            while (came_from.count(current)) {
                path.push_front(current);
                current = came_from[current];
            }
            path.push_front(start);
            return path;
        }

        for (int i = 0; i < D; ++i) {
            for (int dir = -1; dir <= 1; dir += 2) {
                Point neighbor = current;
                neighbor[i] += dir;

                bool out_of_bounds = false;
                for(int j = 0; j < D; ++j) {
                    if (neighbor[j] < 0 || neighbor[j] >= bounds[j]) {
                        out_of_bounds = true;
                        break;
                    }
                }
                if (out_of_bounds || obstacles.count(neighbor)) {
                    continue;
                }

                int tentative_g_score = g_score[current] + 1;
                if (!g_score.count(neighbor) || tentative_g_score < g_score[neighbor]) {
                    came_from[neighbor] = current;
                    g_score[neighbor] = tentative_g_score;
                    int f_score = tentative_g_score + manhattan_distance(neighbor, goal);
                    open_set.push({neighbor, f_score});
                }
            }
        }
    }

    return {}; // No path found
}


int main() {
    std::ios_base::sync_with_stdio(false);
    std::cin.tie(NULL);

    int D, max_turns;
    std::cin >> D >> max_turns;

    std::vector<int> bounds(D);
    for (int i = 0; i < D; ++i) std::cin >> bounds[i];

    int snake_length;
    Point start_pos(D);
    std::cin >> snake_length;
    for (int i = 0; i < D; ++i) std::cin >> start_pos[i];

    std::list<Point> snake;
    snake.push_back(start_pos);

    std::cin >> F;
    std::vector<Point> food(F, Point(D));
    for (int i = 0; i < F; ++i) {
        for (int j = 0; j < D; ++j) {
            std::cin >> food[i][j];
        }
    }

    std::cin >> O;
    ObstacleSet permanent_obstacles;
    for (int i = 0; i < O; ++i) {
        Point obs(D);
        for (int j = 0; j < D; ++j) {
            std::cin >> obs[j];
        }
        permanent_obstacles.insert(obs);
    }

    for (int t = 0; t < max_turns; ++t) {
        Point head = snake.front();

        // Sort food by distance
        std::sort(food.begin(), food.end(), [&](const Point& a, const Point& b) {
            return manhattan_distance(head, a) < manhattan_distance(head, b);
        });

        std::advance(it, 1); // Skip head

            current_obstacles.insert(*it);
        }

            if (!path_to_food.empty()) {
                // Safety check: can we reach our tail after eating?

                std::list<Point> future_snake = snake;

                std::advance(path_it, 1);

                    future_snake.push_front(*path_it);
                }

                    future_snake.pop_back();
                }

                    future_obstacles.insert(p);
                }
                future_obstacles.erase(future_snake.back());

            std::advance(it, 1);
            next_pos = *it;
        } else {
            // Survival mode: move to tail
            if (snake.size() > 1) {
                current_obstacles.erase(snake.back());
            }
            std::list<Point> path_to_tail = a_star_search(head, snake.back(), D, bounds, current_obstacles);
            if (!path_to_tail.empty()) {
                 auto it = path_to_tail.begin();

                 next_pos = *it;

                // Desperation: move to any adjacent free square

                for (int i = 0; i < D; ++i) {

                        Point neighbor = head;
                        neighbor[i] += dir;

                        bool out_of_bounds = false;

                            next_pos = neighbor;
                            moved = true;
                            break;
                        }
                    }
                    if(moved) break;
                }
                 if(!moved) { // Trapped
                    next_pos = head; next_pos[0]++;
                 }
            }
        }

        int move_axis = -1, move_dir = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < D; ++i) {
            if (next_pos[i] != head[i]) {
                move_axis = i;
                move_dir = next_pos[i] - head[i];
                break;
            }
        }

        std::cout << move_axis << " " << move_dir << std::endl;

        snake.push_front(next_pos);

        bool ate_food = false;

            auto food_it = std::find(food.begin(), food.end(), next_pos);

            }
        }

            snake.pop_back();
        }

        if (food.empty()) {
           // All food eaten, just keep surviving
        }
    }

    return 0;
