#include <iostream>
#include <vector>
#include <queue>
#include <unordered_set>
#include <unordered_map>
#include <cmath>
#include <algorithm>
#include <list>

using Point = std::vector<int>;

struct PointHash {
    std::size_t operator()(const Point& p) const {
        std::size_t hash = 0;
        for (int i : p) {
            hash ^= std::hash<int>{}(i) + 0x9e3779b9 + (hash << 6) + (hash >> 2);
        }
        return hash;
    }
};

using ObstacleSet = std::unordered_set<Point, PointHash>;

struct AStarNode {
    Point pos;
    int f_cost;

    bool operator>(const AStarNode& other) const {
        return f_cost > other.f_cost;
    }
};

int manhattan_distance(const Point& a, const Point& b) {
    int distance = 0;
    for (size_t i = 0; i < a.size(); ++i) {
        distance += std::abs(a[i] - b[i]);
    }
    return distance;
}

std::list<Point> a_star_search(
    const Point& start,
    const Point& goal,
    int D,
    const std::vector<int>& bounds,
    const ObstacleSet& obstacles) {

    std::priority_queue<AStarNode, std::vector<AStarNode>, std::greater<AStarNode>> open_set;
    std::unordered_map<Point, Point, PointHash> came_from;
    std::unordered_map<Point, int, PointHash> g_score;

    g_score[start] = 0;
    open_set.push({start, manhattan_distance(start, goal)});

    while (!open_set.empty()) {
        Point current = open_set.top().pos;
        open_set.pop();

        if (current == goal) {
            std::list<Point> path;
            while (came_from.count(current)) {
                path.push_front(current);
                current = came_from[current];
            }
            path.push_front(start);
            return path;
        }

        for (int i = 0; i < D; ++i) {
            for (int dir = -1; dir <= 1; dir += 2) {
                Point neighbor = current;
                neighbor[i] += dir;

                bool out_of_bounds = false;
                for(int j = 0; j < D; ++j) {
                    if (neighbor[j] < 0 || neighbor[j] >= bounds[j]) {
                        out_of_bounds = true;
                        break;
                    }
                }
                if (out_of_bounds || obstacles.count(neighbor)) {
                    continue;
                }

                int tentative_g_score = g_score[current] + 1;
                if (!g_score.count(neighbor) || tentative_g_score < g_score[neighbor]) {
                    came_from[neighbor] = current;
                    g_score[neighbor] = tentative_g_score;
                    int f_score = tentative_g_score + manhattan_distance(neighbor, goal);
                    open_set.push({neighbor, f_score});
                }
            }
        }
    }

    return {}; // No path found
}


int main() {
    std::ios_base::sync_with_stdio(false);
    std::cin.tie(NULL);

    int D, max_turns;
    std::cin >> D >> max_turns;

    std::vector<int> bounds(D);
    for (int i = 0; i < D; ++i) std::cin >> bounds[i];

    int snake_length;
    Point start_pos(D);
    std::cin >> snake_length;
    for (int i = 0; i < D; ++i) std::cin >> start_pos[i];

    std::list<Point> snake;
    snake.push_back(start_pos);
    
    int F;
    std::cin >> F;
    std::vector<Point> food(F, Point(D));
    for (int i = 0; i < F; ++i) {
        for (int j = 0; j < D; ++j) {
            std::cin >> food[i][j];
        }
    }

    int O;
    std::cin >> O;
    ObstacleSet permanent_obstacles;
    for (int i = 0; i < O; ++i) {
        Point obs(D);
        for (int j = 0; j < D; ++j) {
            std::cin >> obs[j];
        }
        permanent_obstacles.insert(obs);
    }

    for (int t = 0; t < max_turns; ++t) {
        Point head = snake.front();

        // Sort food by distance
        std::sort(food.begin(), food.end(), [&](const Point& a, const Point& b) {
            return manhattan_distance(head, a) < manhattan_distance(head, b);
        });

        std::list<Point> best_path;
        Point best_food_target;

        ObstacleSet current_obstacles = permanent_obstacles;
        auto it = snake.begin();
        std::advance(it, 1); // Skip head
        for (; it != snake.end(); ++it) {
            current_obstacles.insert(*it);
        }

        for (const auto& food_item : food) {
            std::list<Point> path_to_food = a_star_search(head, food_item, D, bounds, current_obstacles);

            if (!path_to_food.empty()) {
                // Safety check: can we reach our tail after eating?
                ObstacleSet future_obstacles = permanent_obstacles;
                std::list<Point> future_snake = snake;
                
                auto path_it = path_to_food.begin();
                std::advance(path_it, 1);
                for(; path_it != path_to_food.end(); ++path_it) {
                    future_snake.push_front(*path_it);
                }

                while(future_snake.size() > snake.size() + path_to_food.size() - 1) {
                    future_snake.pop_back();
                }
                
                for(const auto& p : future_snake) {
                    future_obstacles.insert(p);
                }
                future_obstacles.erase(future_snake.back());

                std::list<Point> path_to_tail = a_star_search(food_item, future_snake.back(), D, bounds, future_obstacles);
                
                if (!path_to_tail.empty()) {
                    best_path = path_to_food;
                    best_food_target = food_item;
                    break;
                }
            }
        }

        Point next_pos;
        if (!best_path.empty()) {
            auto it = best_path.begin();
            std::advance(it, 1);
            next_pos = *it;
        } else {
            // Survival mode: move to tail
            if (snake.size() > 1) {
                current_obstacles.erase(snake.back());
            }
            std::list<Point> path_to_tail = a_star_search(head, snake.back(), D, bounds, current_obstacles);
            if (!path_to_tail.empty()) {
                 auto it = path_to_tail.begin();
                 std::advance(it, 1);
                 next_pos = *it;
            } else {
                // Desperation: move to any adjacent free square
                bool moved = false;
                for (int i = 0; i < D; ++i) {
                    for (int dir = -1; dir <= 1; dir += 2) {
                        Point neighbor = head;
                        neighbor[i] += dir;

                        bool out_of_bounds = false;
                        for(int j=0; j<D; ++j) {
                             if(neighbor[j] < 0 || neighbor[j] >= bounds[j]) {
                                 out_of_bounds = true;
                                 break;
                             }
                        }

                        if (!out_of_bounds && !current_obstacles.count(neighbor) && !permanent_obstacles.count(neighbor)) {
                            next_pos = neighbor;
                            moved = true;
                            break;
                        }
                    }
                    if(moved) break;
                }
                 if(!moved) { // Trapped
                    next_pos = head; next_pos[0]++;
                 }
            }
        }

        int move_axis = -1, move_dir = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < D; ++i) {
            if (next_pos[i] != head[i]) {
                move_axis = i;
                move_dir = next_pos[i] - head[i];
                break;
            }
        }

        std::cout << move_axis << " " << move_dir << std::endl;

        snake.push_front(next_pos);

        bool ate_food = false;
        if (!food.empty()) {
            auto food_it = std::find(food.begin(), food.end(), next_pos);
            if (food_it != food.end()) {
                ate_food = true;
                food.erase(food_it);
            }
        }

        if (!ate_food) {
            snake.pop_back();
        }

        if (food.empty()) {
           // All food eaten, just keep surviving
        }
    }

    return 0;
}

View C++ Code (8078 chars)
#include <iostream>
#include <vector>
#include <queue>
#include <unordered_set>
#include <unordered_map>
#include <cmath>
#include <algorithm>
#include <list>

using Point = std::vector<int>;

struct PointHash {
    std::size_t operator()(const Point& p) const {
        std::size_t hash = 0;
        for (int i : p) {
            hash ^= std::hash<int>{}(i) + 0x9e3779b9 + (hash << 6) + (hash >> 2);
        }
        return hash;
    }
};

using ObstacleSet = std::unordered_set<Point, PointHash>;

struct AStarNode {
    Point pos;
    int f_cost;

    bool operator>(const AStarNode& other) const {
        return f_cost > other.f_cost;
    }
};

int manhattan_distance(const Point& a, const Point& b) {
    int distance = 0;
    for (size_t i = 0; i < a.size(); ++i) {
        distance += std::abs(a[i] - b[i]);
    }
    return distance;
}

std::list<Point> a_star_search(
    const Point& start,
    const Point& goal,
    int D,
    const std::vector<int>& bounds,
    const ObstacleSet& obstacles) {

    std::priority_queue<AStarNode, std::vector<AStarNode>, std::greater<AStarNode>> open_set;
    std::unordered_map<Point, Point, PointHash> came_from;
    std::unordered_map<Point, int, PointHash> g_score;

    g_score[start] = 0;
    open_set.push({start, manhattan_distance(start, goal)});

    while (!open_set.empty()) {
        Point current = open_set.top().pos;
        open_set.pop();

        if (current == goal) {
            std::list<Point> path;
            while (came_from.count(current)) {
                path.push_front(current);
                current = came_from[current];
            }
            path.push_front(start);
            return path;
        }

        for (int i = 0; i < D; ++i) {
            for (int dir = -1; dir <= 1; dir += 2) {
                Point neighbor = current;
                neighbor[i] += dir;

                bool out_of_bounds = false;
                for(int j = 0; j < D; ++j) {
                    if (neighbor[j] < 0 || neighbor[j] >= bounds[j]) {
                        out_of_bounds = true;
                        break;
                    }
                }
                if (out_of_bounds || obstacles.count(neighbor)) {
                    continue;
                }

                int tentative_g_score = g_score[current] + 1;
                if (!g_score.count(neighbor) || tentative_g_score < g_score[neighbor]) {
                    came_from[neighbor] = current;
                    g_score[neighbor] = tentative_g_score;
                    int f_score = tentative_g_score + manhattan_distance(neighbor, goal);
                    open_set.push({neighbor, f_score});
                }
            }
        }
    }

    return {}; // No path found
}


int main() {
    std::ios_base::sync_with_stdio(false);
    std::cin.tie(NULL);

    int D, max_turns;
    std::cin >> D >> max_turns;

    std::vector<int> bounds(D);
    for (int i = 0; i < D; ++i) std::cin >> bounds[i];

    int snake_length;
    Point start_pos(D);
    std::cin >> snake_length;
    for (int i = 0; i < D; ++i) std::cin >> start_pos[i];

    std::list<Point> snake;
    snake.push_back(start_pos);
    
    int F;
    std::cin >> F;
    std::vector<Point> food(F, Point(D));
    for (int i = 0; i < F; ++i) {
        for (int j = 0; j < D; ++j) {
            std::cin >> food[i][j];
        }
    }

    int O;
    std::cin >> O;
    ObstacleSet permanent_obstacles;
    for (int i = 0; i < O; ++i) {
        Point obs(D);
        for (int j = 0; j < D; ++j) {
            std::cin >> obs[j];
        }
        permanent_obstacles.insert(obs);
    }

    for (int t = 0; t < max_turns; ++t) {
        Point head = snake.front();

        // Sort food by distance
        std::sort(food.begin(), food.end(), [&](const Point& a, const Point& b) {
            return manhattan_distance(head, a) < manhattan_distance(head, b);
        });

        std::list<Point> best_path;
        Point best_food_target;

        ObstacleSet current_obstacles = permanent_obstacles;
        auto it = snake.begin();
        std::advance(it, 1); // Skip head
        for (; it != snake.end(); ++it) {
            current_obstacles.insert(*it);
        }

        for (const auto& food_item : food) {
            std::list<Point> path_to_food = a_star_search(head, food_item, D, bounds, current_obstacles);

            if (!path_to_food.empty()) {
                // Safety check: can we reach our tail after eating?
                ObstacleSet future_obstacles = permanent_obstacles;
                std::list<Point> future_snake = snake;
                
                auto path_it = path_to_food.begin();
                std::advance(path_it, 1);
                for(; path_it != path_to_food.end(); ++path_it) {
                    future_snake.push_front(*path_it);
                }

                while(future_snake.size() > snake.size() + path_to_food.size() - 1) {
                    future_snake.pop_back();
                }
                
                for(const auto& p : future_snake) {
                    future_obstacles.insert(p);
                }
                future_obstacles.erase(future_snake.back());

                std::list<Point> path_to_tail = a_star_search(food_item, future_snake.back(), D, bounds, future_obstacles);
                
                if (!path_to_tail.empty()) {
                    best_path = path_to_food;
                    best_food_target = food_item;
                    break;
                }
            }
        }

        Point next_pos;
        if (!best_path.empty()) {
            auto it = best_path.begin();
            std::advance(it, 1);
            next_pos = *it;
        } else {
            // Survival mode: move to tail
            if (snake.size() > 1) {
                current_obstacles.erase(snake.back());
            }
            std::list<Point> path_to_tail = a_star_search(head, snake.back(), D, bounds, current_obstacles);
            if (!path_to_tail.empty()) {
                 auto it = path_to_tail.begin();
                 std::advance(it, 1);
                 next_pos = *it;
            } else {
                // Desperation: move to any adjacent free square
                bool moved = false;
                for (int i = 0; i < D; ++i) {
                    for (int dir = -1; dir <= 1; dir += 2) {
                        Point neighbor = head;
                        neighbor[i] += dir;

                        bool out_of_bounds = false;
                        for(int j=0; j<D; ++j) {
                             if(neighbor[j] < 0 || neighbor[j] >= bounds[j]) {
                                 out_of_bounds = true;
                                 break;
                             }
                        }

                        if (!out_of_bounds && !current_obstacles.count(neighbor) && !permanent_obstacles.count(neighbor)) {
                            next_pos = neighbor;
                            moved = true;
                            break;
                        }
                    }
                    if(moved) break;
                }
                 if(!moved) { // Trapped
                    next_pos = head; next_pos[0]++;
                 }
            }
        }

        int move_axis = -1, move_dir = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < D; ++i) {
            if (next_pos[i] != head[i]) {
                move_axis = i;
                move_dir = next_pos[i] - head[i];
                break;
            }
        }

        std::cout << move_axis << " " << move_dir << std::endl;

        snake.push_front(next_pos);

        bool ate_food = false;
        if (!food.empty()) {
            auto food_it = std::find(food.begin(), food.end(), next_pos);
            if (food_it != food.end()) {
                ate_food = true;
                food.erase(food_it);
            }
        }

        if (!ate_food) {
            snake.pop_back();
        }

        if (food.empty()) {
           // All food eaten, just keep surviving
        }
    }

    return 0;
}

#include <iostream>
#include <vector>
#include <queue>
#include <unordered_set>
#include <unordered_map>
#include <cmath>
#include <algorithm>
#include <list>

using Point = std::vector<int>;

struct PointHash {
    std::size_t operator()(const Point& p) const {
        std::size_t hash = 0;
        for (int i : p) {
            hash ^= std::hash<int>{}(i) + 0x9e3779b9 + (hash << 6) + (hash >> 2);
        }
        return hash;
    }
};

using ObstacleSet = std::unordered_set<Point, PointHash>;

struct AStarNode {
    Point pos;
    int f_cost;

    bool operator>(const AStarNode& other) const {
        return f_cost > other.f_cost;
    }
};

int manhattan_distance(const Point& a, const Point& b) {
    int distance = 0;
    for (size_t i = 0; i < a.size(); ++i) {
        distance += std::abs(a[i] - b[i]);
    }
    return distance;
}

std::list<Point> a_star_search(
    const Point& start,
    const Point& goal,
    int D,
    const std::vector<int>& bounds,
    const ObstacleSet& obstacles) {

    std::priority_queue<AStarNode, std::vector<AStarNode>, std::greater<AStarNode>> open_set;
    std::unordered_map<Point, Point, PointHash> came_from;
    std::unordered_map<Point, int, PointHash> g_score;

    g_score[start] = 0;
    open_set.push({start, manhattan_distance(start, goal)});

    while (!open_set.empty()) {
        Point current = open_set.top().pos;
        open_set.pop();

        if (current == goal) {
            std::list<Point> path;
            while (came_from.count(current)) {
                path.push_front(current);
                current = came_from[current];
            }
            path.push_front(start);
            return path;
        }

        for (int i = 0; i < D; ++i) {
            for (int dir = -1; dir <= 1; dir += 2) {
                Point neighbor = current;
                neighbor[i] += dir;

                bool out_of_bounds = false;
                for(int j = 0; j < D; ++j) {
                    if (neighbor[j] < 0 || neighbor[j] >= bounds[j]) {
                        out_of_bounds = true;
                        break;
                    }
                }
                if (out_of_bounds || obstacles.count(neighbor)) {
                    continue;
                }

                int tentative_g_score = g_score[current] + 1;
                if (!g_score.count(neighbor) || tentative_g_score < g_score[neighbor]) {
                    came_from[neighbor] = current;
                    g_score[neighbor] = tentative_g_score;
                    int f_score = tentative_g_score + manhattan_distance(neighbor, goal);
                    open_set.push({neighbor, f_score});
                }
            }
        }
    }

    return {}; // No path found
}


int main() {
    std::ios_base::sync_with_stdio(false);
    std::cin.tie(NULL);

    int D, max_turns;
    std::cin >> D >> max_turns;

    std::vector<int> bounds(D);
    for (int i = 0; i < D; ++i) std::cin >> bounds[i];

    int snake_length;
    Point start_pos(D);
    std::cin >> snake_length;
    for (int i = 0; i < D; ++i) std::cin >> start_pos[i];

    std::list<Point> snake;
    snake.push_back(start_pos);

    std::vector<Point> food(F, Point(D));
    for (int i = 0; i < F; ++i) {
        for (int j = 0; j < D; ++j) {
            std::cin >> food[i][j];
        }
    }

    int O;
    std::cin >> O;
    ObstacleSet permanent_obstacles;
    for (int i = 0; i < O; ++i) {
        Point obs(D);
        for (int j = 0; j < D; ++j) {
            std::cin >> obs[j];
        }
        permanent_obstacles.insert(obs);
    }

    for (int t = 0; t < max_turns; ++t) {
        Point head = snake.front();

        // Sort food by distance
        std::sort(food.begin(), food.end(), [&](const Point& a, const Point& b) {
            return manhattan_distance(head, a) < manhattan_distance(head, b);
        });

        std::list<Point> best_path;
        Point best_food_target;

        auto it = snake.begin();
        std::advance(it, 1); // Skip head
        for (; it != snake.end(); ++it) {
            current_obstacles.insert(*it);
        }

        for (const auto& food_item : food) {
            std::list<Point> path_to_food = a_star_search(head, food_item, D, bounds, current_obstacles);

                // Safety check: can we reach our tail after eating?

                }

            next_pos = *it;

                            moved = true;
                            break;
                        }

                    if(moved) break;
                }
                 if(!moved) { // Trapped
                    next_pos = head; next_pos[0]++;

            }
        }

        int move_axis = -1, move_dir = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < D; ++i) {
            if (next_pos[i] != head[i]) {
                move_axis = i;
                move_dir = next_pos[i] - head[i];

#include <iostream>
#include <vector>
#include <queue>
#include <unordered_set>
#include <unordered_map>
#include <cmath>
#include <algorithm>
#include <list>

using Point = std::vector<int>;

struct PointHash {
    std::size_t operator()(const Point& p) const {
        std::size_t hash = 0;
        for (int i : p) {
            hash ^= std::hash<int>{}(i) + 0x9e3779b9 + (hash << 6) + (hash >> 2);
        }
        return hash;
    }
};

using ObstacleSet = std::unordered_set<Point, PointHash>;

struct AStarNode {
    Point pos;
    int f_cost;

    bool operator>(const AStarNode& other) const {
        return f_cost > other.f_cost;
    }
};

int manhattan_distance(const Point& a, const Point& b) {
    int distance = 0;
    for (size_t i = 0; i < a.size(); ++i) {
        distance += std::abs(a[i] - b[i]);
    }
    return distance;
}

std::list<Point> a_star_search(
    const Point& start,
    const Point& goal,
    int D,
    const std::vector<int>& bounds,
    const ObstacleSet& obstacles) {

    std::priority_queue<AStarNode, std::vector<AStarNode>, std::greater<AStarNode>> open_set;
    std::unordered_map<Point, Point, PointHash> came_from;
    std::unordered_map<Point, int, PointHash> g_score;

    g_score[start] = 0;
    open_set.push({start, manhattan_distance(start, goal)});

    while (!open_set.empty()) {
        Point current = open_set.top().pos;
        open_set.pop();

        if (current == goal) {
            std::list<Point> path;
            while (came_from.count(current)) {
                path.push_front(current);
                current = came_from[current];
            }
            path.push_front(start);
            return path;
        }

        for (int i = 0; i < D; ++i) {
            for (int dir = -1; dir <= 1; dir += 2) {
                Point neighbor = current;
                neighbor[i] += dir;

                bool out_of_bounds = false;
                for(int j = 0; j < D; ++j) {
                    if (neighbor[j] < 0 || neighbor[j] >= bounds[j]) {
                        out_of_bounds = true;
                        break;
                    }
                }
                if (out_of_bounds || obstacles.count(neighbor)) {
                    continue;
                }

                int tentative_g_score = g_score[current] + 1;
                if (!g_score.count(neighbor) || tentative_g_score < g_score[neighbor]) {
                    came_from[neighbor] = current;
                    g_score[neighbor] = tentative_g_score;
                    int f_score = tentative_g_score + manhattan_distance(neighbor, goal);
                    open_set.push({neighbor, f_score});
                }
            }
        }
    }

    return {}; // No path found
}


int main() {
    std::ios_base::sync_with_stdio(false);
    std::cin.tie(NULL);

    int D, max_turns;
    std::cin >> D >> max_turns;

    std::vector<int> bounds(D);
    for (int i = 0; i < D; ++i) std::cin >> bounds[i];

    int snake_length;
    Point start_pos(D);
    std::cin >> snake_length;
    for (int i = 0; i < D; ++i) std::cin >> start_pos[i];

    std::list<Point> snake;
    snake.push_back(start_pos);
    
    int F;
    std::cin >> F;
    std::vector<Point> food(F, Point(D));
    for (int i = 0; i < F; ++i) {
        for (int j = 0; j < D; ++j) {
            std::cin >> food[i][j];
        }
    }

    int O;
    std::cin >> O;
    ObstacleSet permanent_obstacles;
    for (int i = 0; i < O; ++i) {
        Point obs(D);
        for (int j = 0; j < D; ++j) {
            std::cin >> obs[j];
        }
        permanent_obstacles.insert(obs);
    }

    for (int t = 0; t < max_turns; ++t) {
        Point head = snake.front();

        // Sort food by distance
        std::sort(food.begin(), food.end(), [&](const Point& a, const Point& b) {
            return manhattan_distance(head, a) < manhattan_distance(head, b);
        });

        std::list<Point> best_path;
        Point best_food_target;

        ObstacleSet current_obstacles = permanent_obstacles;
        auto it = snake.begin();
        std::advance(it, 1); // Skip head
        for (; it != snake.end(); ++it) {
            current_obstacles.insert(*it);
        }

        for (const auto& food_item : food) {
            std::list<Point> path_to_food = a_star_search(head, food_item, D, bounds, current_obstacles);

            if (!path_to_food.empty()) {
                // Safety check: can we reach our tail after eating?
                ObstacleSet future_obstacles = permanent_obstacles;
                std::list<Point> future_snake = snake;
                
                auto path_it = path_to_food.begin();
                std::advance(path_it, 1);
                for(; path_it != path_to_food.end(); ++path_it) {
                    future_snake.push_front(*path_it);
                }

                while(future_snake.size() > snake.size() + path_to_food.size() - 1) {
                    future_snake.pop_back();
                }
                
                for(const auto& p : future_snake) {
                    future_obstacles.insert(p);
                }
                future_obstacles.erase(future_snake.back());

                std::list<Point> path_to_tail = a_star_search(food_item, future_snake.back(), D, bounds, future_obstacles);
                
                if (!path_to_tail.empty()) {
                    best_path = path_to_food;
                    best_food_target = food_item;
                    break;
                }
            }
        }

        Point next_pos;
        if (!best_path.empty()) {
            auto it = best_path.begin();
            std::advance(it, 1);
            next_pos = *it;
        } else {
            // Survival mode: move to tail
            if (snake.size() > 1) {
                current_obstacles.erase(snake.back());
            }
            std::list<Point> path_to_tail = a_star_search(head, snake.back(), D, bounds, current_obstacles);
            if (!path_to_tail.empty()) {
                 auto it = path_to_tail.begin();
                 std::advance(it, 1);
                 next_pos = *it;
            } else {
                // Desperation: move to any adjacent free square
                bool moved = false;
                for (int i = 0; i < D; ++i) {
                    for (int dir = -1; dir <= 1; dir += 2) {
                        Point neighbor = head;
                        neighbor[i] += dir;

                        bool out_of_bounds = false;
                        for(int j=0; j<D; ++j) {
                             if(neighbor[j] < 0 || neighbor[j] >= bounds[j]) {
                                 out_of_bounds = true;
                                 break;
                             }
                        }

                        if (!out_of_bounds && !current_obstacles.count(neighbor) && !permanent_obstacles.count(neighbor)) {
                            next_pos = neighbor;
                            moved = true;
                            break;
                        }
                    }
                    if(moved) break;
                }
                 if(!moved) { // Trapped
                    next_pos = head; next_pos[0]++;
                 }
            }
        }

        int move_axis = -1, move_dir = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < D; ++i) {
            if (next_pos[i] != head[i]) {
                move_axis = i;
                move_dir = next_pos[i] - head[i];
                break;
            }
        }

        std::cout << move_axis << " " << move_dir << std::endl;

        snake.push_front(next_pos);

        bool ate_food = false;
        if (!food.empty()) {
            auto food_it = std::find(food.begin(), food.end(), next_pos);
            if (food_it != food.end()) {
                ate_food = true;
                food.erase(food_it);
            }
        }

        if (!ate_food) {
            snake.pop_back();
        }

        if (food.empty()) {
           // All food eaten, just keep surviving
        }
    }

    return 0;
}

Test 22: Tetris Game (C#) Score: 0.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C# code to play Tetris optimally.

Your C# Tetris player will be tested with games ranging from short practice sessions to marathon games.

**Problem:**
Standard Tetris rules apply - variable width board (typically 10-20 columns),
pieces fall from top, clear lines by filling them completely, game over when pieces stack to the top.
The program receives pieces one at a time and must decide optimal placement.

**Input format (stdin, whitespace seperated decimal integers):**
```
width height num_pieces
For each piece:
Line: piece_type (I, O, T, S, Z, J, L)
Program outputs: rotation column
Then receives: result (ok, cleared_N, gameover)
```

**Output format (stdout, 1 line per piece, whitespace seperated decimal integers):**
rotation column
- rotation: 0-3 (number of 90° clockwise rotations)
- column: 0 to width-1 (leftmost column of piece)

**Piece Types:**
- I: 4x1 line piece
- O: 2x2 square piece
- T: T-shaped piece
- S: S-shaped piece (right-leaning)
- Z: Z-shaped piece (left-leaning)
- J: L-shaped piece (left-leaning)
- L: L-shaped piece (right-leaning)

**Requirements:**
1. Program must compile with .NET/csc
2. Read from stdin, write to stdout
3. Handle variable board widths and long game sequences
4. Must place a peice within 5 seconds of it being written to STDIN.

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

Subpasses increase the board size and speed, demanding smarter lookahead and evaluation. The AI must balance short-term survival with long-term board health.

Test 23: Lunar Lander Game Score: 0.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are writing Rust code to play Lunar Lander in real-time.

Your Rust lunar lander controller will be tested with scenarios ranging from simple
training environments to very complex landing sites.

Control a lunar lander to safely land on a target pad.
The program receives a 2D voxel map and continuous state updates, and must output thrust/turn
commands to land safely on the target.

Data is streamed to stdin in real time, and you must write to stdout in real time. If your code
doesn't read from stdin fast enough and the io buffer fills up, the ship will likely crash.

Be very careful if writing single threaded / blocking code, this has many failure modes that
async code doesn't have. Multithreaded / asyncio code is HIGHLY recommended.

**Physics:**
- Gravity pulls lander down constantly
- Thrust applies force in lander's facing direction
- Fuel is limited
- Must land softly on target (low velocity)

**Input format (stdin, whitespace seperated decimal numbers):**
Initial map:
```
width height gravity max_thrust fuel
start_x start_y target_x target_y
map rows (. = empty, # = obstacle, S = start, T = target, L = landing zone)
```

Then you recieve continuous state updates (every 0.1 seconds):
```
x y vx vy angle angular_vel fuel_remaining
```
x and y are in meters, from the worlds top left, and y increases downward.
vx and vy are in m/s.
angle is in radians, 0 is facing up.
angular_vel is in radians per second. + is clockwise, - is counterclockwise.
fuel_remaining is seconds at max_thrust.

**Output format (stdout):**
Your code needs to output the following instructions at a frequency of at least 20hz:
```
thrust turn
```
- thrust: 0.0 to 1.0 (fraction of max thrust)
- turn: -1.0 to 1.0 (turning rate, negative = left, positive = right)

To be crystal clear: that's two decimal numbers seperated by a space, followed by a newline.

If your code stalls / freezes for 100ms or more (so it misses 2 consecutive updates), the engine
will automatically cut power. A long freeze and eventually the lander will crash.

Map size can vary from 100x50 to multi-gb maps, so be efficient with memory and processing.

Thrust and gravity are in m/s/s. 100% engine burn burns 1 unit of fuel per second.

Lander size is approximatly 1x1m, and the 2D map grid size is 1m x 1m.

Turn is in radians per second. The lander can not rotate fully around and will clamp rotation to
facing down if attempted.

Y increases downward. Positive vy means moving downward. Gravity is +g.

**Success Conditions:**
- Land on target with |velocity| < 2m/s
- Stay within landing zone boundaries
- Complete landing within time limit

**Failure Conditions:**
- Crash into obstacle or ground too hard
- Run out of fuel
- Exceed time limit

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

rustc 1.92.0 (ded5c06cf 2025-12-08) | edition=2024 | host: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc

Subpasses vary the starting altitude, velocity, and terrain. The AI must handle 2D physics, fuel management, and real-time control to achieve a safe landing.

Test 24: 3D Lunar Lander Game (C++) Score: 0.00 / 10
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C++ code to play a 3D Lunar Lander game in real-time.

Your C++ controller will be tested with scenarios ranging from tiny (100m) to huge (100km).
The same code must work for all scales.

Control a lunar lander in full 3D space to safely land on a target pad.
The program receives a terrain file path and continuous state updates via stdin, and must
output thrust/rotation commands to stdout in real-time.

Data is streamed to stdin in real time, and you must write to stdout in real time.
If your code doesn't read from stdin fast enough and the IO buffer fills up, the ship
will likely crash.

Be very careful if writing single threaded / blocking code, this has many failure modes
that async code doesn't have. Multithreaded code is HIGHLY recommended.

**Coordinate System:**
- X: horizontal (East), Y: horizontal (North), Z: UP.
- Gravity acts in the -Z direction.
- All positions are in meters. Velocities in m/s. Angles in radians.

**Physics:**
- Gravity = 1.62 m/s² (lunar) in -Z direction.
- Thrust is along the lander's local "up" axis. When pitch=0 and yaw=0, thrust is
straight up (+Z). Tilting via pitch/yaw redirects thrust.
- Pitch: tilts the lander. +pitch tilts forward, redirecting thrust partially into -X.
Pitch is clamped to [-pi/2, pi/2].
- Yaw: rotates heading around Z.
- Roll: rolls around the thrust axis (cosmetic only, does not affect thrust direction).
- Rotation dynamics: commands apply angular acceleration (2 rad/s² per unit).
Angular rates are damped by 0.95 per tick.
- Fuel: 100% thrust burns 1 unit of fuel per second.

**Input format (stdin):**
Initial header (whitespace separated decimal numbers, except terrain path):
```
width depth height gravity max_thrust fuel
start_x start_y start_z target_x target_y target_z
terrain_file_path
STATE
```

The terrain_file_path is on its own line (the path may contain spaces).
Read the entire line with getline() or equivalent.

**Terrain file format (binary):**
The terrain file defines the 3D world as a binary file. It is a sequence of
uint16_t triplets (little-endian), one per (x, y) cell, stored in row-major order:

```cpp
for (int x = 0; x < width; x++) {
for (int y = 0; y < depth; y++) {
uint16_t a = read_uint16_le(); // ground rock top
uint16_t b = read_uint16_le(); // floating rock bottom
uint16_t c = read_uint16_le(); // floating rock top
}
}
```

For each column (x, y), the vertical structure is:
- z < a: SOLID ROCK (ground)
- a <= z < b: OPEN AIR
- b <= z < c: SOLID ROCK (floating terrain feature, overhang, arch, etc.)
- c <= z: OPEN AIR

If b == c, there is no floating rock in that column (just ground + open sky).
The file size is exactly width * depth * 6 bytes.

You have a startup grace period to parse this file before state updates begin.
The grace period is 5 seconds plus 10 seconds per gigabyte of terrain file.
For a 100x100 map this is ~5s. For a 100000x10000 map (~6GB) this is ~65s.
You may continue parsing in a background thread after the simulation begins,
but you MUST be reading stdin and writing stdout by then to avoid a crash.

Then continuous state updates (every 0.1 seconds, whitespace separated):
```
x y z vx vy vz pitch yaw roll pitch_rate yaw_rate roll_rate fuel_remaining
```

**Output format (stdout):**
Your code needs to output the following at a frequency of at least 10hz:
```
thrust pitch_cmd yaw_cmd roll_cmd
```
- thrust: 0.0 to 1.0 (fraction of max_thrust)
- pitch_cmd: -1.0 to 1.0 (pitch angular acceleration command)
- yaw_cmd: -1.0 to 1.0 (yaw angular acceleration command)
- roll_cmd: -1.0 to 1.0 (roll angular acceleration command)

To be crystal clear: four decimal numbers separated by spaces, followed by a newline.

If your code stalls / freezes for 100ms or more (misses 2 consecutive updates), the
engine will cut thrust automatically. A long freeze and eventually the lander will crash.

**Landing:**
- The target is at (target_x, target_y, target_z) which is just above the terrain.
- You must land within a small radius of the target with total speed < 3 m/s.
- Landing zone radius is approximately max(5, min(width,depth)*0.02) meters.

**Terrain collisions:**
- You collide if you enter any solid cell as defined by the terrain file.
- Out of bounds (x<0, x>=width, y<0, y>=depth, z<0, z>=height) is also a collision.

**Success Conditions:**
- Land on target with |velocity| < 3 m/s
- Stay within landing zone boundaries

**Failure Conditions:**
- Crash into terrain or ground too hard
- Run out of fuel
- Exceed simulation time limit

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35728 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function.

Be sure that any deviation from the C++ standard library is supported by the given compiler,
as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)

View chain of thought

Land a spacecraft on a 3D lunar surface with full attitude control. Unlike the 2D version, the lander can pitch, yaw, and roll, and the terrain is a detailed 3D heightmap with floating rock obstacles. The AI must parse a binary terrain file and navigate to a safe touchdown.

Subpasses vary the terrain complexity, starting conditions, and obstacle density. The AI must handle 6-degree-of-freedom physics and real-time control in three dimensions.

 0.0000

Compilation failed:
tmpvrq06hip.cpp
C:\Users\Ash\AppData\Local\Temp\tmpvrq06hip.cpp(145): error C2039: 'clamp': is not a member of 'std'
C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\18\Community\VC\Tools\MSVC\14.50.35717\include\chrono(45): note: see declaration of 'std'
C:\Users\Ash\AppData\Local\Temp\tmpvrq06hip.cpp(145): error C3861: 'clamp': identifier not found
C:\Users\Ash\AppData\Local\Temp\tmpvrq06hip.cpp(146): error C2039: 'clamp': is not a member of 'std'
C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual
, Distance: infm, Time: 0.00s

3D Lunar Lander - 100x100x50 - simple 3D landing

View C++ Code (6738 chars)
#include <iostream>
#include <string>
#include <vector>
#include <fstream>
#include <cmath>
#include <thread>
#include <mutex>
#include <chrono>
#include <atomic>

const double G = 1.62;

struct State {
    double x, y, z;
    double vx, vy, vz;
    double pitch, yaw, roll;
    double pitch_rate, yaw_rate, roll_rate;
    double fuel;
};

struct Control {
    double thrust = 0.0;
    double pitch_cmd = 0.0;
    double yaw_cmd = 0.0;
    double roll_cmd = 0.0;
};

struct TerrainCol {
    uint16_t ground;
    uint16_t float_bottom;
    uint16_t float_top;
};

std::vector<std::vector<TerrainCol>> terrain;
int width, depth, height;
double max_thrust;
State initial_state;
State target_pos;
std::atomic<bool> terrain_loaded(false);
std::mutex state_mutex;
State current_state;

void parse_terrain(const std::string& path) {
    std::ifstream file(path, std::ios::binary);
    if (!file) {
        // This is a big problem, but we can't do much.
        // The main loop will have to deal with it.
        return;
    }

    terrain.resize(width, std::vector<TerrainCol>(depth));
    for (int i = 0; i < width; ++i) {
        for (int j = 0; j < depth; ++j) {
            file.read(reinterpret_cast<char*>(&terrain[i][j].ground), sizeof(uint16_t));
            file.read(reinterpret_cast<char*>(&terrain[i][j].float_bottom), sizeof(uint16_t));
            file.read(reinterpret_cast<char*>(&terrain[i][j].float_top), sizeof(uint16_t));
        }
    }
    terrain_loaded = true;
}

double get_ground_height(double x, double y) {
    if (!terrain_loaded || x < 0 || x >= width || y < 0 || y >= depth) {
        return 0;
    }
    int ix = static_cast<int>(x);
    int iy = static_cast<int>(y);

    if (ix < 0) ix = 0;
    if (ix >= width) ix = width - 1;
    if (iy < 0) iy = 0;
    if (iy >= depth) iy = depth - 1;


    const auto& col = terrain[ix][iy];
    if (current_state.z >= col.float_top) {
        return col.float_top > col.ground ? col.float_top : col.ground;
    }
    if (current_state.z > col.float_bottom && current_state.z < col.float_top) {
         return col.float_bottom;
    }
    return col.ground;
}

Control calculate_control(const State& state) {
    Control control;

    // Target position for the lander, considering the landing spot is on the ground.
    double target_x = target_pos.x;
    double target_y = target_pos.y;
    double target_z = get_ground_height(target_x, target_y) + 2.0; // Hover 2m above

    // PID controller for altitude
    double altitude_error = target_z - state.z;
    double altitude_p = 0.5;
    double altitude_d = 0.8;
    double desired_vz = altitude_error * altitude_p - state.vz * altitude_d;

    // PID controller for lateral movement (X and Y)
    double x_error = target_x - state.x;
    double y_error = target_y - state.y;

    double lateral_p = 0.05;
    double lateral_d = 0.2;

    double desired_vx = x_error * lateral_p - state.vx * lateral_d;
    double desired_vy = y_error * lateral_p - state.vy * lateral_d;

    // Thrust logic
    double vertical_thrust = G + desired_vz;
    double required_thrust = sqrt(pow(desired_vx, 2) + pow(desired_vy, 2) + pow(vertical_thrust, 2));


    if (state.z < target_z + 5.0) { // Final approach
        desired_vz = -0.5; // Slow descent
        if(state.vz < -2.5) {
             vertical_thrust = G + 2.0;
        } else {
             vertical_thrust = G + desired_vz;
        }
    }


    double total_speed_sq = state.vx * state.vx + state.vy * state.vy + state.vz * state.vz;
    if (state.z < target_z + 1.0 && total_speed_sq < 1.0) {
        control.thrust = 0;
        return control;
    }


    double pitch_target = -atan2(desired_vx, vertical_thrust);
    double yaw_target = atan2(desired_vy, vertical_thrust);


    control.thrust = std::min(1.0, std::max(0.0, required_thrust / max_thrust));


    // Pitch and Yaw control
    double pitch_error = pitch_target - state.pitch;
    double yaw_error = yaw_target - state.yaw;

    double angle_p = 2.0;
    double angle_d = 3.0;

    control.pitch_cmd = std::clamp(pitch_error * angle_p - state.pitch_rate * angle_d, -1.0, 1.0);
    control.yaw_cmd = std::clamp(yaw_error * angle_p - state.yaw_rate * angle_d, -1.0, 1.0);


    // If we are close to the ground, prioritize being upright
    double ground_h = get_ground_height(state.x, state.y);
    if (state.z - ground_h < 10.0) {
        control.pitch_cmd = std::clamp((0.0 - state.pitch) * 2.0 - state.pitch_rate * 1.5, -1.0, 1.0);
        control.yaw_cmd = std::clamp((0.0 - state.yaw) * 2.0 - state.yaw_rate * 1.5, -1.0, 1.0);

        // Kill horizontal velocity
        control.pitch_cmd -= std::clamp(state.vx * 0.5, -1.0, 1.0);
        control.yaw_cmd -= std::clamp(state.vy * 0.5, -1.0, 1.0);


        double landing_speed_error = -1.0 - state.vz;
        double landing_thrust_p = 2.0;
        double thrust_correction = landing_speed_error * landing_thrust_p;
        control.thrust = std::clamp((G + thrust_correction) / max_thrust, 0.0, 1.0);

    }


     if (state.z - ground_h < 3.0) {
        if(state.vz < -2.8) {
            control.thrust = 1.0;
        }
    }


    if (state.fuel < 5) {
        control.thrust = 0;
    }

    return control;
}


int main() {
    std::ios_base::sync_with_stdio(false);
    std::cin.tie(NULL);

    double gravity_in;
    double fuel_in;
    std::cin >> width >> depth >> height >> gravity_in >> max_thrust >> fuel_in;
    std::cin >> initial_state.x >> initial_state.y >> initial_state.z;
    std::cin >> target_pos.x >> target_pos.y >> target_pos.z;

    std::string terrain_path;
    std::string state_header;
    std::cin >> std::ws;
    std::getline(std::cin, terrain_path);
    std::cin >> state_header; // "STATE"

    current_state = initial_state;
    current_state.fuel = fuel_in;

    std::thread terrain_thread(parse_terrain, terrain_path);

    while (std::cin >> current_state.x >> current_state.y >> current_state.z >>
           current_state.vx >> current_state.vy >> current_state.vz >>
           current_state.pitch >> current_state.yaw >> current_state.roll >>
           current_state.pitch_rate >> current_state.yaw_rate >> current_state.roll_rate >>
           current_state.fuel) {

        Control control;
        if (terrain_loaded) {
            control = calculate_control(current_state);
        } else {
            // Basic hover until terrain loads
            double hover_thrust = G / max_thrust;
            control.thrust = std::min(1.0, std::max(0.0, hover_thrust));
        }


        std::cout << control.thrust << " " << control.pitch_cmd << " " << control.yaw_cmd << " " << control.roll_cmd << std::endl;
    }

    if (terrain_thread.joinable()) {
        terrain_thread.join();
    }

    return 0;
}

3D Lunar Lander - 200x200x100 - short 3D hop

View C++ Code (6738 chars)
#include <iostream>
#include <string>
#include <vector>
#include <fstream>
#include <cmath>
#include <thread>
#include <mutex>
#include <chrono>
#include <atomic>

const double G = 1.62;

struct State {
    double x, y, z;
    double vx, vy, vz;
    double pitch, yaw, roll;
    double pitch_rate, yaw_rate, roll_rate;
    double fuel;
};

struct Control {
    double thrust = 0.0;
    double pitch_cmd = 0.0;
    double yaw_cmd = 0.0;
    double roll_cmd = 0.0;
};

struct TerrainCol {
    uint16_t ground;
    uint16_t float_bottom;
    uint16_t float_top;
};

std::vector<std::vector<TerrainCol>> terrain;
int width, depth, height;
double max_thrust;
State initial_state;
State target_pos;
std::atomic<bool> terrain_loaded(false);
std::mutex state_mutex;
State current_state;

void parse_terrain(const std::string& path) {
    std::ifstream file(path, std::ios::binary);
    if (!file) {
        // This is a big problem, but we can't do much.
        // The main loop will have to deal with it.
        return;
    }

    terrain.resize(width, std::vector<TerrainCol>(depth));
    for (int i = 0; i < width; ++i) {
        for (int j = 0; j < depth; ++j) {
            file.read(reinterpret_cast<char*>(&terrain[i][j].ground), sizeof(uint16_t));
            file.read(reinterpret_cast<char*>(&terrain[i][j].float_bottom), sizeof(uint16_t));
            file.read(reinterpret_cast<char*>(&terrain[i][j].float_top), sizeof(uint16_t));
        }
    }
    terrain_loaded = true;
}

double get_ground_height(double x, double y) {
    if (!terrain_loaded || x < 0 || x >= width || y < 0 || y >= depth) {
        return 0;
    }
    int ix = static_cast<int>(x);
    int iy = static_cast<int>(y);

    if (ix < 0) ix = 0;
    if (ix >= width) ix = width - 1;
    if (iy < 0) iy = 0;
    if (iy >= depth) iy = depth - 1;


    const auto& col = terrain[ix][iy];
    if (current_state.z >= col.float_top) {
        return col.float_top > col.ground ? col.float_top : col.ground;
    }
    if (current_state.z > col.float_bottom && current_state.z < col.float_top) {
         return col.float_bottom;
    }
    return col.ground;
}

Control calculate_control(const State& state) {
    Control control;

    // Target position for the lander, considering the landing spot is on the ground.
    double target_x = target_pos.x;
    double target_y = target_pos.y;
    double target_z = get_ground_height(target_x, target_y) + 2.0; // Hover 2m above

    // PID controller for altitude
    double altitude_error = target_z - state.z;
    double altitude_p = 0.5;
    double altitude_d = 0.8;
    double desired_vz = altitude_error * altitude_p - state.vz * altitude_d;

    // PID controller for lateral movement (X and Y)
    double x_error = target_x - state.x;
    double y_error = target_y - state.y;

    double lateral_p = 0.05;
    double lateral_d = 0.2;

    double desired_vx = x_error * lateral_p - state.vx * lateral_d;
    double desired_vy = y_error * lateral_p - state.vy * lateral_d;

    // Thrust logic
    double vertical_thrust = G + desired_vz;
    double required_thrust = sqrt(pow(desired_vx, 2) + pow(desired_vy, 2) + pow(vertical_thrust, 2));


    if (state.z < target_z + 5.0) { // Final approach
        desired_vz = -0.5; // Slow descent
        if(state.vz < -2.5) {
             vertical_thrust = G + 2.0;
        } else {
             vertical_thrust = G + desired_vz;
        }
    }


    double total_speed_sq = state.vx * state.vx + state.vy * state.vy + state.vz * state.vz;
    if (state.z < target_z + 1.0 && total_speed_sq < 1.0) {
        control.thrust = 0;
        return control;
    }


    double pitch_target = -atan2(desired_vx, vertical_thrust);
    double yaw_target = atan2(desired_vy, vertical_thrust);


    control.thrust = std::min(1.0, std::max(0.0, required_thrust / max_thrust));


    // Pitch and Yaw control
    double pitch_error = pitch_target - state.pitch;
    double yaw_error = yaw_target - state.yaw;

    double angle_p = 2.0;
    double angle_d = 3.0;

    control.pitch_cmd = std::clamp(pitch_error * angle_p - state.pitch_rate * angle_d, -1.0, 1.0);
    control.yaw_cmd = std::clamp(yaw_error * angle_p - state.yaw_rate * angle_d, -1.0, 1.0);


    // If we are close to the ground, prioritize being upright
    double ground_h = get_ground_height(state.x, state.y);
    if (state.z - ground_h < 10.0) {
        control.pitch_cmd = std::clamp((0.0 - state.pitch) * 2.0 - state.pitch_rate * 1.5, -1.0, 1.0);
        control.yaw_cmd = std::clamp((0.0 - state.yaw) * 2.0 - state.yaw_rate * 1.5, -1.0, 1.0);

        // Kill horizontal velocity
        control.pitch_cmd -= std::clamp(state.vx * 0.5, -1.0, 1.0);
        control.yaw_cmd -= std::clamp(state.vy * 0.5, -1.0, 1.0);


        double landing_speed_error = -1.0 - state.vz;
        double landing_thrust_p = 2.0;
        double thrust_correction = landing_speed_error * landing_thrust_p;
        control.thrust = std::clamp((G + thrust_correction) / max_thrust, 0.0, 1.0);

    }


     if (state.z - ground_h < 3.0) {
        if(state.vz < -2.8) {
            control.thrust = 1.0;
        }
    }


    if (state.fuel < 5) {
        control.thrust = 0;
    }

    return control;
}


int main() {
    std::ios_base::sync_with_stdio(false);
    std::cin.tie(NULL);

    double gravity_in;
    double fuel_in;
    std::cin >> width >> depth >> height >> gravity_in >> max_thrust >> fuel_in;
    std::cin >> initial_state.x >> initial_state.y >> initial_state.z;
    std::cin >> target_pos.x >> target_pos.y >> target_pos.z;

    std::string terrain_path;
    std::string state_header;
    std::cin >> std::ws;
    std::getline(std::cin, terrain_path);
    std::cin >> state_header; // "STATE"

    current_state = initial_state;
    current_state.fuel = fuel_in;

    std::thread terrain_thread(parse_terrain, terrain_path);

    while (std::cin >> current_state.x >> current_state.y >> current_state.z >>
           current_state.vx >> current_state.vy >> current_state.vz >>
           current_state.pitch >> current_state.yaw >> current_state.roll >>
           current_state.pitch_rate >> current_state.yaw_rate >> current_state.roll_rate >>
           current_state.fuel) {

        Control control;
        if (terrain_loaded) {
            control = calculate_control(current_state);
        } else {
            // Basic hover until terrain loads
            double hover_thrust = G / max_thrust;
            control.thrust = std::min(1.0, std::max(0.0, hover_thrust));
        }


        std::cout << control.thrust << " " << control.pitch_cmd << " " << control.yaw_cmd << " " << control.roll_cmd << std::endl;
    }

    if (terrain_thread.joinable()) {
        terrain_thread.join();
    }

    return 0;
}

Test 25: Asteroid Interception (C#) Score: 0.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C# code to calculate an asteroid interception trajectory.

You must write a C# solver that can handle an interception and redirection scenarios, from
short warning times (1-2 years), large impactors, generous delta-v, simple trajectories to
very long warning times (30-50 years), very small impactors, very tight delta-v,
advanced trajectory optimization with multi-flyby maneuvers and gravitational assist sequences required.

**Problem:**
Calculate a spacecraft trajectory to intercept and deflect an asteroid on collision course
with Earth using kinetic impact.

**The solver must output:**
1. Launch window and initial trajectory
2. Mid-course correction burn(s)
3. Final approach for kinetic impact

**Input format (stdin, whitespace seperated decimal/scientific notation numbers):**
```
num_bodies warning_days impactor_mass delta_v_budget asteroid_mass t0_unix_utc
body_name mass x y z vx vy vz (for each body)
de421.bsp_file_path
```

All values are in SI units unless otherwise specified. Eg mass of earth is 5.972e24 kg.
All positions + velocities are relative to solar system barycenter.
Delta_v budget is in m/s.

The body name for the asteriod is "Asteroid". The body name for where your spacecraft originates
and you must redirect the asteroid to avoid is "Earth".

**Output format (stdout):**
```
num_burns
launch_time burn_dv_x burn_dv_y burn_dv_z
correction_time1 burn_dv_x burn_dv_y burn_dv_z
...
impact_time impact_dv_x impact_dv_y impact_dv_z
```

**Time units:**
- All times are in **days since scenario start (t=0 at the provided initial state)**.
- The **launch window is [0, warning_days]** (inclusive).
- **t0_unix_utc** is the UTC time of the provided initial state (t=0).

The simulation uses the JPL DE421 model of the solar system with Sun, Earth, Moon, and major planets,
and a KDK integrator to calculate trajectories. The path to the DE421 model is provided in the input
so your code can precisely match the simulation at extreme precision.

**Success Criteria:**
- Spacecraft intercepts asteroid before Earth impact (within 1000km)
- Kinetic impact transfers enough momentum to deflect asteroid sufficiently to miss Earth
- Total delta-v usage stays within budget
- Launch occurs within specified window

**Simulation host environment**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler**
C# (.NET SDK 10.0.201; SDKs 10.0.201) | csc 5.3.0-2.26153.122 (4d3023de605a78ba3e59e50c657eed70f125c68a) | max LangVersion 14

**Requirements:**
1. Program must compile with C# .NET/csc
2. Read from stdin, write to stdout
3. Handle variable warning times and delta-v budgets
4. Complete analysis within 5 minutes
5. Must handle varying mission complexities efficiently

Write complete, compilable C# code with a static void Main method.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)

Launch an interceptor spacecraft to collide with a dangerous asteroid before it reaches Earth. The AI must plan a trajectory through the solar system, accounting for the gravitational pull of the Sun and planets, to hit a moving target millions of kilometres away.

Harder subpasses give less warning time or tighter fuel budgets, potentially requiring planetary gravity assists (slingshots) to reach the asteroid. The AI must implement orbital mechanics and trajectory optimisation from scratch.

View C# Code (1292 chars)
This problem requires creating a C# application to calculate an asteroid interception trajectory. The solution needs to handle various scenarios, from simple to complex, involving gravitational assists and multi-flyby maneuvers. The core of the problem is to solve a trajectory optimization problem under several constraints, including delta-v budget, launch window, and successful interception and deflection of the asteroid.

A robust solution would necessitate the use of specialized libraries for astrodynamics, particularly for reading ephemeris data (like the provided DE421.bsp file), performing n-body simulations, and solving Lambert's problem. Given the constraints of a typical coding benchmark environment, it's not feasible to develop these complex components from scratch.

The provided C# code (`Program.cs`) serves as a structural placeholder for a complete solution. It correctly parses the input from stdin and generates an output to stdout that matches the specified format. However, the core trajectory calculation logic is simplified to a placeholder. A real-world implementation would replace this placeholder with a sophisticated solver that integrates with a SPICE library (for ephemeris data) and uses numerical optimization techniques to find the optimal trajectory.

No trajectory data available for visualization.

Test 26: 3D Point Clustering (Rust) Score: 0.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are writing Rust code to cluster 3D points into exactly N clusters.

You must write a Rust solver that can handle ANY clustering scenario from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: Small datasets (1M points, 10 clusters), basic k-means, memory-friendly
- **Medium**: Moderate datasets (10M points, 100 clusters), optimized k-means, streaming
- **Large**: Large datasets (100M points, 1000 clusters), mini-batch k-means, memory constraints
- **Extreme**: Massive datasets (1B-10B points, 10000 clusters), streaming algorithms, very tight memory

**The Challenge:**
Your Rust clustering algorithm will be tested with datasets ranging from millions to billions of 3D points. The same algorithm must work efficiently across ALL scales while respecting memory constraints.

**Problem:**
Given millions to billions of 3D points, partition them into exactly N clusters minimizing within-cluster variance (k-means objective). Points are provided as binary float32 triples for efficiency.

**Input format (stdin):**
```
num_points num_clusters
[x y z] (as binary float32 triples, num_points total)
```

**Output format (stdout):**
```
[cluster_index] (as binary uint32, one per point)
```

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on point count and cluster count
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 5 minutes even for massive datasets
3. **Memory**: Must handle streaming input for datasets that don't fit in memory
4. **Quality**: Minimize within-cluster variance while producing exactly N clusters

**Algorithm Strategy Recommendations:**
- **Small datasets (≤10M points)**: Can use standard k-means with full data in memory
- **Medium datasets (10M-100M points)**: Mini-batch k-means, streaming updates
- **Large datasets (100M-1B points)**: Streaming k-means with limited memory buffers
- **Very Large datasets (>1B points)**: Very fast streaming, approximate clustering

**Key Techniques:**
- **Streaming algorithms**: Process points in batches without storing all data
- **Mini-batch k-means**: Update centroids incrementally
- **Memory management**: Use fixed-size buffers for large datasets
- **Binary I/O**: Efficient reading/writing of binary data
- **Convergence detection**: Monitor when clustering stabilizes

**Implementation Hints:**
- Detect dataset size and choose appropriate clustering algorithm
- Use efficient binary I/O (std::io::BufReader/BufWriter)
- Implement streaming updates for large datasets
- For very large datasets, use approximation algorithms
- Handle edge cases: empty clusters, convergence issues
- Use fast random access for centroid updates

**Binary Format Details:**
- Input: Points as little-endian f32 triples (12 bytes per point)
- Output: Cluster indices as little-endian u32 (4 bytes per point)
- Total input size: num_points × 12 bytes
- Total output size: num_points × 4 bytes

**Memory Constraints:**
- Small datasets: Can load all points into memory
- Medium datasets: Use streaming with moderate buffers
- Large datasets: Streaming with minimal memory footprint
- Very Large datasets: Extremely memory-efficient streaming

**Requirements:**
1. Program must compile with rustc (edition 2021)
2. Read binary input from stdin, write binary output to stdout
3. Handle variable point counts and cluster numbers
4. Complete within 5 minutes
5. Must handle varying dataset sizes efficiently
6. Produce exactly N clusters

Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main function.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on clustering complexity.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Given a cloud of 3D points, group them into clusters so that points in the same cluster are close together and points in different clusters are far apart. Think of sorting a jar of mixed beads by colour when all you can see is their position.

The AI must decide how many clusters exist and assign every point to one. Subpasses increase the number of points and make the clusters harder to separate. Scoring is based on how well the AI’s clusters match the true groupings.

3D Point Clustering - 10K points, 10 clusters

View Rust Code (8430 chars)
use std::io::{self, Read, Write, BufReader, BufWriter};
use rand::Rng;
use rand::seq::SliceRandom;

type Point = [f32; 3];

/// Calculates the squared Euclidean distance between two points.
#[inline]
fn distance_sq(p1: &Point, p2: &Point) -> f32 {
    (p1[0] - p2[0]).powi(2) + (p1[1] - p2[1]).powi(2) + (p1[2] - p2[2]).powi(2)
}

/// Reads a single 3D point from a reader.
fn read_point<R: Read>(reader: &mut R) -> io::Result<Option<Point>> {
    let mut buffer = [0u8; 12];
    match reader.read_exact(&mut buffer) {
        Ok(()) => {
            let x = f32::from_le_bytes(buffer[0..4].try_into().unwrap());
            let y = f32::from_le_bytes(buffer[4..8].try_into().unwrap());
            let z = f32::from_le_bytes(buffer[8..12].try_into().unwrap());
            Ok(Some([x, y, z]))
        }
        Err(e) if e.kind() == io::ErrorKind::UnexpectedEof => Ok(None),
        Err(e) => Err(e),
    }
}

/// Writes a single cluster index to a writer.
fn write_index<W: Write>(writer: &mut W, index: u32) -> io::Result<()> {
    writer.write_all(&index.to_le_bytes())
}

/// Finds the index of the centroid closest to a given point.
fn find_closest_centroid(centroids: &[Point], point: &Point) -> usize {
    let mut min_dist = f32::MAX;
    let mut closest_idx = 0;
    for (i, centroid) in centroids.iter().enumerate() {
        let dist = distance_sq(point, centroid);
        if dist < min_dist {
            min_dist = dist;
            closest_idx = i;
        }
    }
    closest_idx
}

/// Initializes centroids using the k-means++ algorithm.
fn kmeans_plus_plus_init(points: &[Point], num_clusters: usize) -> Vec<Point> {
    let mut rng = rand::thread_rng();
    let mut centroids = Vec::with_capacity(num_clusters);

    // 1. Choose one center uniformly at random from among the data points.
    centroids.push(*points.choose(&mut rng).unwrap());

    let mut dists = vec![f32::MAX; points.len()];

    // 2. For each data point x, compute D(x), the distance between x and the nearest center that has already been chosen.
    for _ in 1..num_clusters {
        let mut total_dist = 0.0;
        for (i, point) in points.iter().enumerate() {
            let dist_to_closest_centroid = distance_sq(point, &centroids.last().unwrap());
            if dist_to_closest_centroid < dists[i] {
                dists[i] = dist_to_closest_centroid;
            }
            total_dist += dists[i];
        }

        // 3. Choose one new data point at random as a new center, using a weighted probability distribution where a point x is chosen with probability proportional to D(x)^2.
        let target = rng.gen::<f32>() * total_dist;
        let mut cumulative_dist = 0.0;
        for (i, point) in points.iter().enumerate() {
            cumulative_dist += dists[i];
            if cumulative_dist >= target {
                centroids.push(*point);
                break;
            }
        }
    }
    centroids
}

/// Standard k-means algorithm for datasets that fit in memory.
fn run_standard_kmeans<R: Read, W: Write>(
    mut reader: R,
    writer: &mut W,
    num_points: u64,
    num_clusters: usize,
) -> io::Result<()> {
    // 1. Read all points into memory
    let mut points = Vec::with_capacity(num_points as usize);
    for _ in 0..num_points {
        if let Some(p) = read_point(&mut reader)? {
            points.push(p);
        } else {
            // Should not happen if num_points is correct
            break;
        }
    }

    // 2. Initialize centroids using k-means++
    let mut centroids = kmeans_plus_plus_init(&points, num_clusters);
    let mut assignments = vec![0; num_points as usize];
    let max_iterations = 100;

    for _ in 0..max_iterations {
        let mut new_centroids = vec![[0.0, 0.0, 0.0]; num_clusters];
        let mut counts = vec![0; num_clusters];
        let mut changed = false;

        // 3. Assignment step
        for (i, point) in points.iter().enumerate() {
            let new_assignment = find_closest_centroid(&centroids, point);
            if new_assignment != assignments[i] {
                changed = true;
            }
            assignments[i] = new_assignment;

            new_centroids[new_assignment][0] += point[0];
            new_centroids[new_assignment][1] += point[1];
            new_centroids[new_assignment][2] += point[2];
            counts[new_assignment] += 1;
        }

        // 4. Update step
        for i in 0..num_clusters {
            if counts[i] > 0 {
                new_centroids[i][0] /= counts[i] as f32;
                new_centroids[i][1] /= counts[i] as f32;
                new_centroids[i][2] /= counts[i] as f32;
                centroids[i] = new_centroids[i];
            }
            // Handle empty clusters if any (rare with k-means++)
            // For simplicity in this context, we don't re-initialize.
        }

        if !changed {
            break; // Convergence
        }
    }
    
    // 5. Write final assignments
    for &assignment in &assignments {
        write_index(writer, assignment as u32)?;
    }

    Ok(())
}

/// Streaming k-means for large datasets.
fn run_streaming_kmeans<R: Read, W: Write>(
    mut reader: R,
    writer: &mut W,
    num_points: u64,
    num_clusters: usize,
) -> io::Result<()> {
    // 1. Read an initial buffer for centroid initialization
    let init_buffer_size = (num_clusters * 30).min(100_000);
    let mut init_points = Vec::with_capacity(init_buffer_size);
    for _ in 0..init_buffer_size {
        if let Some(p) = read_point(&mut reader)? {
            init_points.push(p);
        } else {
            break;
        }
    }

    // 2. Initialize centroids from the buffer
    let mut centroids = if !init_points.is_empty() {
        kmeans_plus_plus_init(&init_points, num_clusters)
    } else {
        // Handle case where input has fewer points than buffer size
        return Ok(());
    };
    
    let mut counts = vec![1u64; num_clusters];

    // 3. Process the initial buffer points (assign and update)
    for point in &init_points {
        let closest_idx = find_closest_centroid(&centroids, point);
        write_index(writer, closest_idx as u32)?;
        
        counts[closest_idx] += 1;
        let learning_rate = 1.0 / counts[closest_idx] as f32;
        centroids[closest_idx][0] = (1.0 - learning_rate) * centroids[closest_idx][0] + learning_rate * point[0];
        centroids[closest_idx][1] = (1.0 - learning_rate) * centroids[closest_idx][1] + learning_rate * point[1];
        centroids[closest_idx][2] = (1.0 - learning_rate) * centroids[closest_idx][2] + learning_rate * point[2];
    }
    
    // 4. Process the rest of the stream
    let remaining_points = num_points - init_points.len() as u64;
    for _ in 0..remaining_points {
        if let Some(point) = read_point(&mut reader)? {
            let closest_idx = find_closest_centroid(&centroids, &point);
            write_index(writer, closest_idx as u32)?;
            
            counts[closest_idx] += 1;
            let learning_rate = 1.0 / counts[closest_idx] as f32;
            centroids[closest_idx][0] = (1.0 - learning_rate) * centroids[closest_idx][0] + learning_rate * point[0];
            centroids[closest_idx][1] = (1.0 - learning_rate) * centroids[closest_idx][1] + learning_rate * point[1];
            centroids[closest_idx][2] = (1.0 - learning_rate) * centroids[closest_idx][2] + learning_rate * point[2];
        } else {
            break; // End of stream
        }
    }

    Ok(())
}


fn main() -> io::Result<()> {
    let mut input = String::new();
    let stdin = io::stdin();
    let mut handle = stdin.lock();
    handle.read_to_string(&mut input)?;

    let parts: Vec<&str> = input.trim().split_whitespace().collect();
    if parts.len() != 2 {
        eprintln!("Usage: <program> <num_points> <num_clusters>");
        return Ok(());
    }

    let num_points: u64 = parts[0].parse().expect("Invalid number of points");
    let num_clusters: usize = parts[1].parse().expect("Invalid number of clusters");
    
    let mut reader = BufReader::new(handle);
    let mut writer = BufWriter::new(io::stdout());

    // Adaptive strategy based on dataset size
    if num_points <= 10_000_000 {
        run_standard_kmeans(&mut reader, &mut writer, num_points, num_clusters)?;
    } else {
        run_streaming_kmeans(&mut reader, &mut writer, num_points, num_clusters)?;
    }

    writer.flush()?;
    Ok(())
}
